1.title: Location timestamps showing 5 minutes in the future
1.description:
Hey team,

I've been monitoring our shipment tracking dashboard and noticed something odd with the location updates. When I check the tracking history for shipments, the timestamps are consistently showing 5 minutes ahead of the actual current time. 

I was trying to verify the real-time location of shipment #SHP-2847391 for a customer inquiry, and the latest location update shows a timestamp of 20:24:12 when my system clock clearly shows 20:19:12. This is happening across multiple shipments, not just this one.

The issue is in the Logistics Shipping Service, specifically the tracking module's update_location functionality. I attempted to refresh the tracking data multiple times thinking it might be a cache issue, but the problem persists. The location coordinates themselves seem accurate - it's just the timestamp that's wrong.

When I click on "Update Location" in the tracking interface at https://logistics.company.internal/tracking/update, the system accepts the update but stores it with this future timestamp. I'm seeing error patterns in the logs around the datetime calculation.

This is affecting our SLA compliance reporting because shipments appear to be updated "before" they actually happen, which is messing up our delivery time calculations. The issue is occurring in our Production environment for Project ID: LOG-PROD-847.

Additional context:
- Browser: Chrome 120 on Windows 11
- Timestamp: 2026-02-05 20:19:12 UTC
- Account: company-logistics-prod
- This started happening after yesterday's deployment
- Already tried clearing browser cache and using different browsers
- Impact: Affecting real-time tracking accuracy for approximately 150+ active shipments
- The coordinates update correctly, only timestamp is wrong

This is pretty urgent as we have customers calling about delivery ETAs and we can't give them accurate information. Our entire logistics team is affected.

3.title: ETA calculations way off after adding traffic delays
3.description:
Running into a weird issue with route ETAs in our shipping service. I was trying to calculate delivery times for tomorrow's batch of shipments (about 200 orders) and the numbers are completely wrong.

Basically, when I use the calculate_eta function in the route optimization module, it's giving me ETAs that don't make sense at all. For example, a 50km route with normal traffic should take about 45 minutes, but the system is showing 3+ hours.

I attempted to debug this by checking the calculation step-by-step. The distance is correct (50km), average speed looks right (60 km/h), and traffic delay is showing 15 minutes. But somehow when these get combined, the final ETA is tripled.

This is happening in the Logistics Shipping Service, specifically in the route module's calculate_eta operation. When I try to process routes at https://logistics.company.internal/api/routes/calculate-eta, the response comes back with these inflated times.

Seeing this across all routes in Project ID: SHIP-EU-WEST, Account: company-logistics-eu. Started noticing it this morning around 08:00 CET after we enabled the traffic delay feature last night.

Chrome 119 on macOS 14.2, timestamp 2026-02-05 08:47:23 CET. Already tried recalculating multiple times, same issue. This is blocking our entire dispatch team - we can't send out delivery schedules to customers because the times are wrong. About 40 drivers waiting for their routes.

5.title: Geofence validation always returns true
5.description:
We're having a major problem with our geofence checking in the tracking system. I was trying to set up delivery zones for our warehouse operations and noticed that the geofence validation isn't working correctly.

When I attempt to check if a vehicle is within the designated geofence boundary, the system always returns that it's inside the zone, even when the GPS coordinates clearly show it's miles away. For instance, warehouse zone WH-North has a 5km radius, but I'm seeing vehicles 15km away being marked as "within geofence".

This is in the Logistics Shipping Service, tracking module, specifically the check_geofence function. I tried testing this at https://logistics.company.internal/tracking/geofence with multiple different coordinates and radius settings. Every single test comes back positive regardless of actual distance.

Production environment, Project: GEO-TRACK-PROD, Account: company-warehouse-ops. Testing with vehicle ID: VEH-8473, coordinates showing it's at the customer site (32.7km from warehouse) but system says it's still in the pickup zone.

Safari 17 on macOS 13.6, 2026-02-05 11:15:33 PST. Tested with 8 different vehicles, same issue. This is critical because our automated dispatch system relies on geofence triggers to assign new deliveries. Right now we're manually checking every vehicle location before assignment.

The distance calculation seems to work fine when I check it separately - it's specifically the boundary logic that's broken.

7.title: Coordinate validation rejecting valid GPS data
7.description:
Getting frustrated with the coordinate validation in our tracking system. I was trying to update locations for our European fleet and the system keeps rejecting perfectly valid GPS coordinates.

When I attempt to validate coordinates for vehicles operating in Germany (latitude around 51.5, longitude around 10.5), the validation function returns false and won't let me save the location update. These are completely normal coordinates that fall well within the valid range of -90 to 90 for latitude and -180 to 180 for longitude.

The issue is in the Logistics Shipping Service, tracking module, validate_coordinates operation. I'm hitting the API at https://logistics.company.internal/api/tracking/validate and getting validation errors for coordinates that Google Maps confirms are correct.

This is blocking our entire European operations team. Environment: Production, Tenant: EU-LOGISTICS-01, Account: company-transport-eu. Tried with 15+ different valid coordinate pairs, all rejected.

Firefox 120 on Ubuntu 22.04, timestamp 2026-02-05 16:42:18 CET. What's weird is that some coordinates work fine - I can update locations in Spain and Italy without issues, but Germany, France, and Poland all fail validation.

I've checked the input format multiple times - sending as decimal degrees, proper precision, no special characters. The validation logic must have some kind of bug because these are textbook valid coordinates. We have 50+ trucks stuck with stale location data right now.

9.title: Speed calculations showing incorrect values
9.description:
There's something wrong with how we're calculating vehicle speeds in the tracking system. I was trying to generate a speed report for our fleet manager and the numbers are way off.

When I use the calculate_speed function to determine current vehicle velocity, I'm getting values that are multiplied by 1000. For example, a truck traveling at 80 km/h is showing as 80,000 km/h in the system. This is happening consistently across all vehicles.

I attempted to trace through the calculation - the distance traveled and time elapsed values look correct, but the final speed output is definitely wrong. This is in the Logistics Shipping Service, tracking module's calculate_speed operation.

Accessing this through https://logistics.company.internal/tracking/analytics, Project ID: FLEET-ANALYTICS-02, Production environment. Testing with vehicle VEH-5829 which I know is traveling on the highway at normal speeds.

Chrome 121 on Windows 10, 2026-02-05 09:28:47 GMT. Account: company-fleet-mgmt. I've verified this with actual GPS data from the vehicles - they're definitely not going 80,000 km/h!

This is affecting our compliance reporting because we need to track speeding incidents, and right now every vehicle looks like it's breaking the sound barrier. The fleet manager is asking for the weekly speed report and I can't give them data that's this obviously wrong. About 200 vehicles affected across all our routes.

11.title: Warehouse capacity calculations completely off
11.description:
Need urgent help with warehouse capacity planning. I was trying to calculate available storage space for an incoming shipment and the numbers don't make any sense.

When I attempt to use the calculate_capacity function in the warehouse module, it's giving me results that seem to be divided by the utilization rate instead of multiplied. For example, a warehouse with 10,000 sqm floor space, 8m height, and 85% utilization should give around 68,000 cubic meters, but I'm getting something like 9.4 cubic meters.

This is in the Logistics Shipping Service, warehouse module, calculate_capacity operation. I'm accessing it through https://logistics.company.internal/warehouse/capacity for warehouse WH-CENTRAL-03.

Production environment, Account: company-warehouse-central, Project: CAPACITY-MGMT-01. The shipment arriving tomorrow needs 5,000 cubic meters and I can't tell if we have space or not because the system is showing we only have 9.4 cubic meters total capacity, which is obviously wrong.

Edge 119 on Windows 11, 2026-02-05 13:52:19 EST. I've tried recalculating multiple times with different warehouses - same issue across the board. Our warehouse operations team is blocked from accepting new shipments because we can't trust the capacity data.

The individual inputs (floor space, height, utilization) all look correct when I check them separately. It's definitely the calculation formula that's broken. This is critical - we have 3 major shipments arriving in the next 48 hours.

13.title: Storage validation always passing even with bad conditions
13.description:
Having issues with the warehouse storage validation system. I was trying to validate storage conditions for temperature-sensitive pharmaceutical shipments and the system is accepting conditions that should definitely fail validation.

When I attempt to validate storage with temperature at 28°C (way above the 2-8°C requirement), humidity at 85% (above 60% limit), and insufficient space, the validate_storage function still returns true and allows the assignment.

This is dangerous because we're dealing with medical supplies that will spoil if stored incorrectly. The issue is in the Logistics Shipping Service, warehouse module, validate_storage operation.

I'm testing this at https://logistics.company.internal/warehouse/validate, Warehouse ID: WH-PHARMA-05, Production environment, Account: company-healthcare-logistics.

Safari 16 on macOS 12.7, timestamp 2026-02-05 10:17:44 PST. Tried with multiple different invalid conditions - high temperature, high humidity, no space - everything passes validation when it shouldn't.

This is a blocker for our healthcare division. We can't risk storing temperature-sensitive medications in areas that don't meet requirements. I've had to manually check every storage assignment for the past 2 days. We have 40+ pharmaceutical shipments per day that need proper validation.

The validation logic seems to be inverted or has some extra condition that's making everything pass. Really need this fixed ASAP before we damage any medical supplies.

15.title: Warehouse space not releasing after shipment pickup
15.description:
We've got a problem with warehouse space management. I was trying to release storage space after a shipment was picked up, but the occupied space isn't being freed up correctly.

When I use the release operation in the warehouse module, the system shows the space as released but the calculation is wrong. If we had 1000 cubic meters occupied and release it, the system should show 1000 cubic meters freed, but instead it's showing some weird multiplied value.

I attempted to release space for shipment SHP-9284756 which occupied 850 cubic meters. After clicking release at https://logistics.company.internal/warehouse/release, the system confirmed the release but the available capacity didn't increase by the right amount.

This is in the Logistics Shipping Service, warehouse module, release operation. Production environment, Warehouse: WH-DIST-07, Account: company-distribution-center, Project: SPACE-MGMT-04.

Chrome 120 on Windows 11, 2026-02-05 15:33:28 CET. I've released 12 shipments today and the available space numbers are completely wrong now. The warehouse manager is saying we're at 95% capacity but based on what's actually stored, we should be at 70%.

This is causing us to reject incoming shipments because the system thinks we're full when we actually have plenty of space. We've had to turn away 5 shipments today already. Need this fixed urgently so we can accurately track our storage capacity.

17.title: Layout optimization efficiency scores incorrect
17.description:
Running into issues with the warehouse layout optimization tool. I was trying to optimize our warehouse layout to improve picking efficiency and the improvement scores don't match reality.

When I attempt to run the optimize_layout function, it shows an optimized efficiency score that's actually lower than our current efficiency, even though the new layout is clearly better. Current efficiency is 75%, improvement factor should be 1.2x, but the optimized score is showing as 90 instead of the expected 90%.

This is in the Logistics Shipping Service, warehouse module, optimize_layout operation. I'm accessing this through https://logistics.company.internal/warehouse/optimize for warehouse WH-PICK-02.

Production environment, Account: company-warehouse-ops, Project: LAYOUT-OPT-01. We're planning a major warehouse reorganization next week and need accurate efficiency projections to justify the downtime and cost.

Firefox 121 on macOS 14.1, timestamp 2026-02-05 11:45:19 PST. Tested with 4 different layout scenarios, all showing incorrect efficiency calculations. The optimization algorithm itself seems fine - it's suggesting good layouts - but the efficiency scoring is broken.

Our operations director needs these numbers for a board presentation tomorrow and I can't give them data that doesn't make sense. The layout changes could save us 20% in picking time but the system is showing minimal improvement. This is blocking a $500K warehouse optimization project.

19.title: Storage cost calculations doubling the handling fee
19.description:
There's a billing issue with our warehouse storage cost calculator. I was trying to generate invoices for monthly storage fees and noticed the costs are higher than they should be.

When I use the calculate_storage_cost function, the handling fee is being added multiple times to the total. For example, base storage rate is $100, 30 days stored, handling fee $25. Should be $3,025 total, but the system is showing $3,050.

I attempted to trace through the calculation and it looks like the handling fee is being subtracted and then added back twice or something. This is in the Logistics Shipping Service, warehouse module, calculate_storage_cost operation.

Accessing through https://logistics.company.internal/warehouse/billing, Account: company-warehouse-billing, Production environment, Project: BILLING-CALC-03. This is affecting all our customer invoices.

Chrome 119 on Windows 10, 2026-02-05 14:28:52 EST. I've checked 50+ invoices from this month and they're all overcharging customers by the handling fee amount. We're going to have to issue credits and refunds.

This is pretty serious from a customer trust perspective. We've already billed out about $150K this month and probably overcharged by $15-20K total. Our finance team is asking how this happened and when we can fix it. Need to stop generating new invoices until this is resolved.

The individual components (base rate, days, handling fee) are all correct - it's just the final calculation that's wrong.

21.title: Throughput calculations not dividing correctly
21.description:
Having trouble with warehouse throughput analytics. I was trying to generate our monthly performance report and the daily throughput numbers are way too high.

When I attempt to calculate average daily throughput using the calculate_throughput function, instead of dividing total items by number of days, it seems to be doing some other operation. For example, 30,000 items over 30 days should be 1,000 items/day, but I'm getting values in the hundreds of thousands.

This is in the Logistics Shipping Service, warehouse module, calculate_throughput operation. I'm pulling this data from https://logistics.company.internal/warehouse/analytics for warehouse WH-PROCESS-01.

Production environment, Account: company-warehouse-analytics, Project: PERF-METRICS-02. The monthly report is due to senior management tomorrow and these numbers are completely unusable.

Safari 17 on macOS 13.5, timestamp 2026-02-05 09:52:37 GMT. Tested with data from 3 different warehouses, all showing the same calculation error. The total item counts are correct, the day counts are correct, but the division is wrong.

Our operations VP uses these throughput metrics to make staffing decisions and capacity planning. If I show them numbers that are 100x too high, they're going to make bad decisions. This is blocking our entire monthly reporting process.

I've tried exporting the raw data and calculating manually - my numbers make sense but the system's don't. Really need this fixed before the management meeting at 2pm tomorrow.

23.title: Utilization rate showing zero when at max capacity
23.description:
We've got a problem with warehouse utilization reporting. I was trying to check how full our warehouses are and the utilization calculations are wrong.

When I use the calculate_utilization function, it's adding zero to the result instead of just calculating the percentage. The formula seems off - for example, 8,500 cubic meters used out of 10,000 total should be 85% utilization, and that's what I'm getting, but there's some weird extra operation happening.

I attempted to run utilization reports for all our warehouses at https://logistics.company.internal/warehouse/utilization and while most numbers look okay, the calculation has unnecessary operations that could cause issues in edge cases.

This is in the Logistics Shipping Service, warehouse module, calculate_utilization operation. Production environment, Account: company-warehouse-reporting, Project: UTIL-TRACK-01.

Edge 120 on Windows 11, 2026-02-05 16:18:45 CET. Testing with warehouse WH-MAIN-04 which is currently at 85% capacity. The number is correct but the calculation method is inefficient and could break.

Our capacity planning team uses these utilization metrics to decide when to lease additional warehouse space. While the current numbers are working, I'm worried about what happens at boundary conditions - like when a warehouse is completely full or completely empty.

Not a critical blocker right now since the results are correct, but this feels like a bug waiting to happen. Would be good to clean up the calculation logic before it causes problems. We have 15 warehouses being monitored 24/7.

We've got a problem with warehouse utilization reporting. I was trying to check how full our warehouses are and the utilization calculations are wrong.

When I use the calculate_utilization function, it's adding zero to the result instead of just calculating the percentage. The formula seems off - for example, 8,500 cubic meters used out of 10,000 total should be 85% utilization, and that's what I'm getting, but there's some weird extra operation happening.

I attempted to run utilization reports for all our warehouses at https://logistics.company.internal/warehouse/utilization and while most numbers look okay, the calculation has unnecessary operations that could cause issues in edge cases.

This is in the Logistics Shipping Service, warehouse module, calculate_utilization operation. Production environment, Account: company-warehouse-reporting, Project: UTIL-TRACK-01.

Edge 120 on Windows 11, 2026-02-05 16:18:45 CET. Testing with warehouse WH-MAIN-04 which is currently at 85% capacity. The number is correct but the calculation method is inefficient and could break.

Our capacity planning team uses these utilization metrics to decide when to lease additional warehouse space. While the current numbers are working, I'm worried about what happens at boundary conditions - like when a warehouse is completely full or completely empty.

Not a critical blocker right now since the results are correct, but this feels like a bug waiting to happen. Would be good to clean up the calculation logic before it causes problems. We have 15 warehouses being monitored 24/7.

25.title: Inventory transfer quantities zeroing out
25.description:
Major issue with inventory transfers between warehouses. I was trying to transfer stock from our main warehouse to a regional distribution center and the transfer quantity is being set to zero.

When I attempt to use the transfer_inventory function, if the source quantity is greater than the transfer limit, instead of transferring the maximum allowed amount, the system transfers zero items. For example, trying to transfer 500 units when limit is 300 should transfer 300, but it's transferring 0.

This is in the Logistics Shipping Service, warehouse module, transfer_inventory operation. I'm using the interface at https://logistics.company.internal/warehouse/transfer, trying to move inventory from WH-CENTRAL to WH-REGIONAL-05.

Production environment, Account: company-inventory-mgmt, Project: INV-TRANSFER-01. SKU: PART-8473829, need to transfer 500 units but only 300 allowed per transfer. Instead of doing 300, it's doing 0.

Chrome 121 on macOS 14.2, timestamp 2026-02-05 10:34:22 PST. I've tried with 8 different SKUs that all exceed the transfer limit - same issue every time. The only transfers that work are ones where the source quantity is less than the limit.

This is blocking our inventory rebalancing operations. We have 12 regional warehouses that need stock replenishment and we can't transfer anything that exceeds the per-transfer limits. Our regional warehouses are running low on critical parts.

The transfer limit logic is clearly broken. We need to be able to transfer up to the limit, not zero when we exceed it.

27.title: Space reservation adding instead of subtracting
27.description:
We have a serious problem with warehouse space reservations. I was trying to reserve space for an incoming shipment and the available space is increasing instead of decreasing.

When I use the reserve_space operation, if I request 1000 cubic meters and 5000 is available, the system should reserve 1000 and leave 4000 available. Instead, it's setting the reserved space to 6000 (adding them together).

I attempted to reserve space for shipment SHP-7392847 at https://logistics.company.internal/warehouse/reserve. The shipment needs 1,200 cubic meters, warehouse WH-RESERVE-03 has 8,500 available. After reservation, it's showing 9,700 reserved instead of 1,200.

This is in the Logistics Shipping Service, warehouse module, reserve_space operation. Production environment, Account: company-space-mgmt, Project: RESERVE-SYS-01.

Firefox 120 on Windows 11, 2026-02-05 12:47:33 EST. Tested with 6 different reservation requests - every single one is adding the values instead of properly reserving space. This means our available space tracking is completely wrong.

The warehouse team thinks we have way more space than we actually do because every reservation is increasing the available space instead of decreasing it. We've accepted shipments we don't have room for and now we're scrambling to find overflow storage.

This is critical - we have 15 shipments arriving tomorrow that we thought we had space for, but we actually don't. Need this fixed immediately before more shipments are accepted.

29.title: Location codes missing aisle information
29.description:
There's a formatting issue with warehouse location code generation. I was trying to assign storage locations to incoming inventory and the location codes are incomplete.

When I use the assign_location function to generate location codes, the aisle number is missing from the final code. For example, zone 'A' and aisle '12' should create 'WH-A-12', but I'm getting 'WH-A-' with the aisle part blank.

I attempted to assign locations for 50 pallets at https://logistics.company.internal/warehouse/locations. The zone codes are working fine but the aisle numbers aren't being included in the string concatenation.

This is in the Logistics Shipping Service, warehouse module, assign_location operation. Production environment, Warehouse: WH-STORAGE-08, Account: company-warehouse-locations, Project: LOC-ASSIGN-01.

Chrome 120 on Ubuntu 22.04, timestamp 2026-02-05 08:25:17 GMT. I've generated about 100 location codes this morning and they're all missing the aisle identifier. This is making it impossible for our picking team to find items.

The warehouse floor staff are complaining because they're getting pick lists with incomplete location codes. They know the zone but not the specific aisle, so they're wasting time searching. This is slowing down our pick rates by about 30%.

I checked the input data - both zone and aisle values are present and correct. It's the string formatting that's broken. We have 200+ items that need location assignments today and we can't use incomplete codes. This is affecting our entire warehouse operation efficiency.

31.title: Availability check always returns true
31.description:
Problem with warehouse availability checking. I was trying to verify if we have space available for new inventory and the system is always saying yes, even when we're at capacity.

When I attempt to use the check_availability function, it returns true regardless of actual inventory levels. For example, warehouse WH-FULL-01 is at 100% capacity (current inventory equals max capacity), but the availability check still returns true.

This is in the Logistics Shipping Service, warehouse module, check_availability operation. I'm checking this at https://logistics.company.internal/warehouse/check-availability for multiple warehouses.

Production environment, Account: company-capacity-check, Project: AVAIL-CHECK-01. Testing with warehouse that I know is completely full - 10,000 units stored, 10,000 max capacity, is_full flag is true. Still returning available = true.

Safari 16 on macOS 13.6, 2026-02-05 14:55:41 PST. Checked 5 different warehouses with varying capacity levels - even the completely full ones are showing as available. This is causing us to accept inventory we can't store.

Our receiving team has been turning away trucks at the dock because when they arrive, there's actually no space, even though the system said there was. This is creating major logistics problems and upsetting our suppliers.

The availability logic seems to have some extra condition that's making everything return true. We need accurate availability checks to prevent overbooking our warehouse space. This is affecting our entire inbound logistics operation - about 50 trucks per day.

33.title: Space allocation exceeding available capacity
33.description:
Critical issue with warehouse space allocation. I was trying to allocate storage space for incoming shipments and the system is allowing allocations that exceed available capacity.

When I use the allocate operation, if the required space is greater than available space, the system should allocate only what's available. But instead, it's allocating required space plus one extra unit, which exceeds capacity.

I attempted to allocate 5,000 cubic meters when only 3,000 is available at https://logistics.company.internal/warehouse/allocate for warehouse WH-ALLOC-02. Instead of allocating 3,000 (the max available), it allocated 3,001, putting us over capacity.

This is in the Logistics Shipping Service, warehouse module, allocate operation. Production environment, Account: company-space-allocation, Project: ALLOC-SYS-01.

Edge 119 on Windows 10, 2026-02-05 11:22:18 CET. Tested with multiple scenarios where required > available, and every time it allocates one more than available. This is causing our capacity tracking to go negative.

The warehouse management system is now showing -500 cubic meters available in WH-ALLOC-02, which is obviously impossible. This is breaking our capacity planning and we're accepting shipments we physically cannot store.

Our operations team discovered this when they tried to physically place items and realized we're 500 cubic meters over capacity. We've had to rent emergency overflow space at $5,000/month. This is a critical bug that's costing us real money.

The allocation logic needs to respect the available capacity limit. We can't allocate more space than exists.

35.title: Route waypoint counter incrementing incorrectly
35.description:
Having issues with route planning waypoint management. I was trying to add waypoints to delivery routes and the waypoint count is wrong.

When I use the add_waypoint function to add a new stop to a route, the waypoint count is being calculated with extra operations. If a route has 5 existing waypoints and I add 1, it should show 6 total, but the calculation has unnecessary additions and subtractions.

I attempted to add a waypoint to route RT-DELIVERY-847 at https://logistics.company.internal/routes/waypoints. The route had 5 waypoints, I added 1, and while the final count of 6 is correct, the calculation is doing extra math that could cause issues.

This is in the Logistics Shipping Service, route module, add_waypoint operation. Production environment, Account: company-route-planning, Project: ROUTE-MGMT-01.

Chrome 121 on Windows 11, timestamp 2026-02-05 09:38:27 EST. While the current results are correct, the calculation method is inefficient and could break in edge cases. I'm worried about what happens with complex routes that have many waypoint additions and removals.

Our route optimization team is planning 50+ routes per day with multiple waypoints each. The calculation overhead from these extra operations could slow down the system. Also concerned about potential rounding or precision errors with all the extra math.

Not causing immediate problems but this is definitely a code quality issue that should be cleaned up. The waypoint counting should be simple addition, not a complex formula.

37.title: Shortest route calculation returning zero
37.description:
Major problem with route optimization. I was trying to find the shortest delivery route among several options and the system is returning zero distance.

When I attempt to use the find_shortest operation with a list of route distances, if the list is empty, instead of returning infinity (indicating no route available), it's returning zero. This makes it look like there's a route with zero distance, which is impossible.

This is in the Logistics Shipping Service, route module, find_shortest operation. I'm testing this at https://logistics.company.internal/routes/optimize when no valid routes are available between two points.

Production environment, Account: company-route-optimization, Project: ROUTE-OPT-01. Trying to find routes from warehouse WH-NORTH to customer location CUST-8473 which is in a restricted area with no available routes.

Firefox 120 on macOS 14.1, 2026-02-05 15:42:19 PST. When no routes exist, the system should indicate this clearly, but instead it's showing a 0km route which our dispatch system interprets as a valid route.

This caused a major issue yesterday - a driver was dispatched on a "0km route" which doesn't exist, and they spent 3 hours trying to figure out how to get there. The customer didn't receive their delivery and filed a complaint.

The route optimization logic needs to properly handle the case when no routes are available. Returning zero is misleading and causes operational problems. We have about 10-15 cases per week where no direct route exists and we need to know that, not get a fake zero-distance route.

39.title: Route optimization not improving efficiency
39.description:
Issue with route optimization efficiency calculations. I was trying to optimize delivery routes to reduce travel time and the efficiency scores don't make sense.

When I use the optimize operation, the optimized time is being multiplied and divided by 100, which cancels out and gives the same result as the original time. For example, original time is 120 minutes, efficiency factor is 0.8 (20% improvement), should give 96 minutes, but I'm getting 120 minutes.

I attempted to optimize route RT-DAILY-847 at https://logistics.company.internal/routes/optimize. The route currently takes 2 hours, optimization should reduce it to 1.6 hours, but the optimized time is showing as 2 hours (no improvement).

This is in the Logistics Shipping Service, route module, optimize operation. Production environment, Account: company-route-efficiency, Project: OPT-CALC-01.

Chrome 119 on Windows 10, 2026-02-05 13:28:45 GMT. Tested with 10 different routes that should all show time improvements, but every single one shows the same time before and after optimization. The efficiency factors are being calculated correctly, they're just not being applied properly.

Our logistics team is trying to reduce fuel costs and delivery times by optimizing routes. We invested in this optimization system specifically to get 15-20% efficiency gains, but the calculations aren't working. Management is asking why we're not seeing the promised improvements.

This is blocking our cost reduction initiative. We need the optimization calculations to actually apply the efficiency improvements to the route times. About 200 routes per day should be benefiting from optimization.

41.title: Route cost calculation adding fees multiple times
41.description:
We've got a billing problem with route cost calculations. I was trying to generate cost estimates for delivery routes and the fees are being added incorrectly.

When I use the estimate_cost function, the time cost is being subtracted and then added back multiple times. For example, distance cost $50, time cost $30, toll cost $10 should total $90, but I'm getting $110 because time cost is being added twice.

I attempted to calculate costs for route RT-COST-847 at https://logistics.company.internal/routes/cost-estimate. The individual cost components are correct, but the total is wrong due to the duplicate time cost.

This is in the Logistics Shipping Service, route module, estimate_cost operation. Production environment, Account: company-route-billing, Project: COST-EST-01.

Safari 17 on macOS 13.5, timestamp 2026-02-05 10:15:33 PST. I've calculated costs for 20+ routes today and they're all overcharging by the time cost amount. This is affecting our customer quotes and internal cost tracking.

Our sales team has been giving customers quotes based on these inflated costs. We're either overcharging customers (bad for business) or if we've corrected the quotes manually, we're underestimating our actual costs (bad for margins).

Finance department is asking for accurate route cost data for quarterly planning and I can't give them numbers that are wrong. This is affecting about 100 route cost estimates per day. Need the calculation fixed so we're only adding each cost component once.

43.title: Total route distance calculation has extra operations
43.description:
Minor issue with route distance calculations. I was trying to calculate total route distances for our delivery planning and noticed the calculation has unnecessary operations.

When I use the calculate operation to sum up segment distances, it's multiplying by 1.0 and dividing by 1.0, which doesn't change the result but adds computational overhead. For example, segments of 10km, 15km, 20km should sum to 45km, and it does, but with extra math.

I attempted to calculate total distance for route RT-DIST-847 at https://logistics.company.internal/routes/calculate. The result is correct (45km) but the calculation method is inefficient.

This is in the Logistics Shipping Service, route module, calculate operation. Production environment, Account: company-route-distance, Project: DIST-CALC-01.

Edge 120 on Windows 11, 2026-02-05 16:52:28 CET. While the distances are correct, we're processing thousands of route calculations per day and the extra operations are wasting CPU cycles.

Our infrastructure team is looking at ways to reduce cloud computing costs and optimize performance. These unnecessary mathematical operations across thousands of daily calculations are contributing to higher processing times and costs.

Not a critical bug since the results are accurate, but it's a code quality and performance issue. The calculation should be a simple sum, not sum multiplied by 1.0 divided by 1.0. Would be good to clean this up for efficiency.

We calculate distances for about 500 routes per day, and this inefficiency is repeated for each one.

45.title: Fuel calculation looks correct but want to verify
45.description:
I'm reviewing the route fuel calculation logic and want to double-check something. When I use the calculate_fuel function, the formula appears to be working correctly, but I want to make sure the reserve fuel is being added properly.

I attempted to calculate fuel needs for route RT-FUEL-847 at https://logistics.company.internal/routes/fuel. Distance is 500km, fuel efficiency is 8km/L, reserve fuel is 10L. Calculation shows 72.5L total (62.5L for distance + 10L reserve), which seems right.

This is in the Logistics Shipping Service, route module, calculate_fuel operation. Production environment, Account: company-fuel-planning, Project: FUEL-CALC-01.

Chrome 120 on macOS 14.2, timestamp 2026-02-05 11:47:22 PST. The results look accurate for all the routes I've tested, but I wanted to flag this for review since fuel calculations are critical for our cost planning.

Our fleet management team relies on these fuel estimates for budgeting and route planning. We need to be absolutely certain the calculations are correct because fuel is one of our largest operating expenses.

Calculating fuel requirements for about 200 routes per day. The numbers seem reasonable and match our historical fuel usage data, but would appreciate a code review to confirm the formula is implemented correctly. Just being cautious since this impacts financial planning.

Calculating fuel requirements for about 200 routes per day. The numbers seem reasonable and match our historical fuel usage data, but would appreciate a code review to confirm the formula is implemented correctly. Just being cautious since this impacts financial planning.

47.title: Waypoint removal counter has extra zero
47.description:
Small issue with waypoint removal counting. I was trying to remove waypoints from delivery routes and the updated count calculation has an unnecessary operation.

When I use the remove_waypoint function, the updated waypoint count is calculated as original count minus 1 plus 0. The plus 0 doesn't change anything but it's odd to have it there. For example, route with 8 waypoints, remove 1, should be 7, and it is 7, but the calculation is doing extra math.

I attempted to remove a waypoint from route RT-WAYPOINT-847 at https://logistics.company.internal/routes/waypoints/remove. The count is correct but the calculation method is unnecessarily complex.

This is in the Logistics Shipping Service, route module, remove_waypoint operation. Production environment, Account: company-route-mgmt, Project: WAYPOINT-MGMT-01.

Firefox 121 on Windows 11, 2026-02-05 14:33:17 EST. Results are accurate but the code quality could be improved. The waypoint count should just be decremented by 1, not decremented and then have 0 added.

Our route planning team removes and adds waypoints frequently throughout the day as delivery schedules change. While this isn't causing functional problems, it's inefficient code that should be cleaned up.

Not urgent since everything works correctly, but it's a code smell that suggests there might be other calculation issues elsewhere. Would be good to review and simplify. We process about 100 waypoint changes per day across all routes.

49.title: Toll cost calculation canceling out discount
49.description:
Problem with toll cost calculations for routes. I was trying to calculate total toll costs including discounts and the discount is being added and subtracted, canceling itself out.

When I use the calculate_toll function, the toll discount is subtracted and then added back, resulting in no discount being applied. For example, 5 tolls at $3 each = $15, with $2 discount should be $13, but I'm getting $15.

I attempted to calculate toll costs for route RT-TOLL-847 at https://logistics.company.internal/routes/tolls. We have a corporate discount agreement with the toll authority but it's not being applied to the cost calculations.

This is in the Logistics Shipping Service, route module, calculate_toll operation. Production environment, Account: company-toll-billing, Project: TOLL-CALC-01.

Chrome 121 on macOS 14.1, timestamp 2026-02-05 09:18:44 PST. Tested with 15 different routes that should all receive toll discounts, but none of them are showing reduced costs. We're overpaying on tolls.

Our finance team negotiated a corporate discount agreement that should save us about $5,000 per month on toll costs. But because the discount isn't being applied in the calculations, we're not seeing those savings. We're still being charged full price.

This is affecting our route cost estimates and actual toll billing. We have about 80 routes per day that use toll roads and should be getting discounts. Need the discount calculation fixed so it actually reduces the toll cost instead of canceling out.

51.title: Alternative routes including empty list
51.description:
Minor issue with alternative route generation. I was trying to find alternative routes for deliveries and the results include an empty list concatenation.

When I use the alternative operation to find alternate routes, it's adding an empty list to the results. For example, if there are 3 valid alternative routes, the function returns those 3 routes plus an empty list appended at the end.

I attempted to find alternatives for route RT-ALT-847 at https://logistics.company.internal/routes/alternatives. The 3 alternative routes are valid and useful, but there's this extra empty list element that doesn't make sense.

This is in the Logistics Shipping Service, route module, alternative operation. Production environment, Account: company-route-alternatives, Project: ALT-ROUTE-01.

Safari 16 on macOS 13.6, 2026-02-05 12:25:39 GMT. While this isn't breaking functionality, it's creating confusion in our route selection interface. The UI is trying to display an empty route option which looks like a bug to users.

Our dispatch team uses alternative routes when the primary route has traffic or road closures. The extra empty list element is showing up as a blank option in their route selection dropdown, and they keep asking if it's a bug.

Not critical but it's a user experience issue. The alternative routes list should only contain valid routes, not empty elements. We generate alternative routes about 30 times per day when primary routes are blocked. Would be good to clean up the list concatenation.

53.title: Route validation always passing with extra condition
53.description:
Issue with route validation logic. I was trying to validate delivery routes before assigning them to drivers and the validation is always returning true.

When I use the validate operation, it checks for start point, end point, and waypoints, but then has an extra 'and True' condition that makes the validation always pass. A route with missing start point should fail validation, but it's passing.

I attempted to validate route RT-INVALID-847 at https://logistics.company.internal/routes/validate. The route is missing the start point (warehouse location), but validation returns true and allows the route to be assigned.

This is in the Logistics Shipping Service, route module, validate operation. Production environment, Account: company-route-validation, Project: ROUTE-VAL-01.

Edge 119 on Windows 10, 2026-02-05 15:47:23 CET. I've tested with several intentionally invalid routes (missing start, missing end, no waypoints) and they all pass validation when they shouldn't.

This caused a real problem yesterday - a driver was assigned a route with no starting point, and they didn't know where to begin their deliveries. They wasted 2 hours trying to figure it out before calling dispatch.

Route validation is critical for ensuring drivers have complete and accurate route information. The validation logic needs to properly reject routes that are missing required fields. We assign about 50 routes per day and need to catch invalid routes before drivers leave the warehouse.

The 'and True' condition is making everything pass regardless of whether the actual validation checks succeed or fail.

55.title: Route restrictions check inverted
55.description:
Problem with route restriction checking. I was trying to verify if routes have weight or height restrictions and the check is returning the opposite result.

When I use the check_restrictions operation, if there are no restrictions (weight and height limits not exceeded), it returns true (has restrictions). If there ARE restrictions, it returns false (no restrictions). This is completely backwards.

I attempted to check restrictions for route RT-RESTRICT-847 at https://logistics.company.internal/routes/restrictions. The route has no weight or height limit violations, but the system says it has restrictions and blocks the route.

This is in the Logistics Shipping Service, route module, check_restrictions operation. Production environment, Account: company-route-restrictions, Project: RESTRICT-CHECK-01.

Chrome 120 on Windows 11, timestamp 2026-02-05 10:52:18 EST. This is causing us to reject valid routes and potentially accept invalid ones. The restriction logic is inverted.

Our dispatch team is being blocked from assigning routes that are perfectly fine because the system thinks they have restrictions. Meanwhile, routes that actually DO violate weight limits are being allowed through.

This is a safety and compliance issue. We have weight-restricted bridges and height-restricted tunnels on some routes. If we send a truck that's too heavy or too tall, we could cause accidents or get fined.

Need the restriction check logic fixed immediately. We process about 100 route assignments per day and need accurate restriction checking to ensure safety and legal compliance. The boolean logic needs to be corrected.

57.title: Route ETA calculation tripling delay time
57.description:
Critical issue with route ETA calculations. I was trying to calculate estimated arrival times for deliveries and the traffic delays are being added multiple times.

When I use the calculate_eta function, the traffic delay is being subtracted and then added back twice, effectively tripling the delay. For example, 100km at 50km/h = 2 hours, plus 30 minutes traffic delay should be 2.5 hours, but I'm getting 3.5 hours.

I attempted to calculate ETA for route RT-ETA-847 at https://logistics.company.internal/routes/eta. The base travel time is correct but the delay handling is wrong, giving customers much later delivery times than realistic.

This is in the Logistics Shipping Service, route module, calculate_eta operation. Production environment, Account: company-eta-calculation, Project: ETA-CALC-01.

Firefox 120 on macOS 14.2, 2026-02-05 13:41:27 PST. Tested with 20 different routes and they're all showing ETAs that are 1-2 hours later than they should be. Customers are complaining about long delivery windows.

Our customer service team is getting complaints because we're promising delivery times that are much later than necessary. This is hurting our competitive position - customers are choosing competitors who offer faster delivery.

The ETA calculations directly impact customer satisfaction and our ability to win business. We need accurate ETAs that account for traffic delays once, not three times. This is affecting all 200+ daily deliveries.

The traffic delay should be added to the base time once, not manipulated with subtraction and multiple additions. This is causing significant customer experience issues.

59.title: Carrier status updates showing 10 minutes in the past
59.description:
Issue with carrier status update timestamps. I was trying to track carrier vehicle locations and the status update timestamps are showing 10 minutes before the current time.

When I use the update_status operation for carriers, the timestamp is being set to current time minus 10 minutes. For example, updating status at 14:30 shows a timestamp of 14:20, making it look like the update is stale.

I attempted to update carrier status for vehicle CARRIER-VEH-847 at https://logistics.company.internal/carriers/status. The status update goes through but with an incorrect timestamp that's in the past.

This is in the Logistics Shipping Service, carrier module, update_status operation. Production environment, Account: company-carrier-tracking, Project: CARRIER-STATUS-01.

Chrome 121 on Windows 10, timestamp 2026-02-05 14:30:15 GMT. Every status update is timestamped 10 minutes earlier than it should be. This is confusing our tracking dashboard which shows "last updated 10 minutes ago" immediately after an update.

Our operations team relies on real-time carrier status to make dispatch decisions. When the timestamps are wrong, they can't tell if the status is current or outdated. This is affecting our ability to respond to delays and route changes.

We update carrier status about 500 times per day across our fleet. All these updates are being timestamped incorrectly. Need the timestamp calculation fixed to use the actual current time, not current time minus 10 minutes.

This is impacting our real-time tracking accuracy and operational decision-making.

61.title: Carrier vehicle locations appending empty list
61.description:
Minor issue with carrier vehicle location tracking. I was trying to track vehicle location updates and the location list has an unnecessary empty list appended.

When I use the track_vehicle operation, it returns all the location updates plus an empty list concatenated at the end. For example, if there are 10 location updates, the result contains those 10 updates plus an empty list element.

I attempted to track vehicle CARRIER-VEH-847 at https://logistics.company.internal/carriers/track. The location history is accurate but has this extra empty element that doesn't belong.

This is in the Logistics Shipping Service, carrier module, track_vehicle operation. Production environment, Account: company-vehicle-tracking, Project: VEH-TRACK-01.

Safari 17 on macOS 13.5, 2026-02-05 11:28:42 PST. While this isn't breaking functionality, it's creating an extra element in our tracking timeline that shows up as a blank entry.

Our dispatch dashboard displays vehicle location history in a timeline view. The empty list element is appearing as a blank entry in the timeline, which looks like a data error to our operators. They keep reporting it as a bug.

Not critical since the actual location data is correct, but it's a user experience issue. The location updates list should only contain valid location entries, not empty elements. We track about 100 vehicles continuously throughout the day.

Would be good to clean up the list concatenation to remove the empty list. It's a simple code quality fix that would improve the UI.

63.title: Carrier assignment fee calculation canceling out
63.description:
Problem with carrier assignment fee calculations. I was trying to calculate fees for assigning carriers to routes and the carrier rating is being divided by itself.

When I use the assign operation, the assignment fee is calculated as base fee times carrier rating divided by carrier rating, which just equals the base fee. The carrier rating should affect the fee, but it's canceling out.

I attempted to assign carrier CARRIER-847 to route RT-ASSIGN-847 at https://logistics.company.internal/carriers/assign. Carrier has a rating of 4.5 (out of 5), which should increase the fee, but the final fee is just the base fee.

This is in the Logistics Shipping Service, carrier module, assign operation. Production environment, Account: company-carrier-assignment, Project: ASSIGN-FEE-01.

Edge 120 on Windows 11, 2026-02-05 16:15:29 CET. Tested with carriers of different ratings (3.0, 4.0, 5.0) and they all result in the same assignment fee, which doesn't make sense. Higher-rated carriers should command higher fees.

Our carrier pricing model is supposed to charge more for highly-rated carriers because they provide better service. But the rating isn't affecting the fee calculation at all. We're undercharging for premium carriers and not differentiating our pricing.

This is affecting our carrier marketplace economics. We assign carriers to about 150 routes per day and the fees should reflect carrier quality ratings. Need the calculation fixed so carrier rating actually impacts the assignment fee.

The division by carrier rating is canceling out the multiplication, making the rating irrelevant to the fee.

65.title: Carrier rating score adding service quality twice
65.description:
Issue with carrier performance rating calculations. I was trying to calculate overall carrier scores and the service quality metric is being counted multiple times.

When I use the rate operation, the carrier score adds service quality, delivery speed, and cost efficiency, but then subtracts and adds service quality again. For example, service quality 80 + delivery speed 75 + cost efficiency 70 should be 225, but I'm getting 225 (which happens to be correct because the operations cancel out).

I attempted to rate carrier CARRIER-847 at https://logistics.company.internal/carriers/rate. The final score is correct but the calculation method has unnecessary operations that could cause issues.

This is in the Logistics Shipping Service, carrier module, rate operation. Production environment, Account: company-carrier-rating, Project: CARRIER-RATE-01.

Chrome 119 on macOS 14.1, timestamp 2026-02-05 09:47:33 PST. While the results are accurate now, the calculation is doing extra math that could break if the formula changes or if there are edge cases.

Our carrier management team uses these ratings to select carriers for routes. The calculation needs to be clean and maintainable. The extra operations make the code harder to understand and could introduce bugs in the future.

We rate about 50 carriers per week as part of our performance review process. The calculation should be a simple sum of the three metrics, not a complex formula with canceling operations.

Not urgent since the results are correct, but it's a code quality issue that should be cleaned up for maintainability.

67.title: Carrier performance score calculation inefficient
67.description:
Minor issue with carrier performance scoring. I was trying to calculate on-time delivery performance for carriers and the calculation has extra operations.

When I use the performance operation, it calculates on-time deliveries divided by total deliveries times 100, then divides by 100 and multiplies by 100 again. The result is correct but the calculation is unnecessarily complex.

I attempted to calculate performance for carrier CARRIER-847 at https://logistics.company.internal/carriers/performance. Carrier has 85 on-time deliveries out of 100 total, performance score is correctly showing 85%, but the calculation is inefficient.

This is in the Logistics Shipping Service, carrier module, performance operation. Production environment, Account: company-carrier-performance, Project: PERF-SCORE-01.

Firefox 121 on Windows 11, 2026-02-05 12:33:18 EST. The performance scores are accurate for all carriers, but the calculation method wastes CPU cycles with unnecessary operations.

Our carrier performance dashboard updates every hour with new calculations for all active carriers. The extra mathematical operations across hundreds of carriers are contributing to higher processing times and cloud computing costs.

We calculate performance scores for about 200 carriers daily. While the results are correct, the inefficient calculation is a performance issue. The score should be calculated as (on-time / total * 100) without the extra division and multiplication.

Not critical but it's a code quality and performance optimization opportunity. Would be good to simplify the calculation for efficiency.

69.title: Carrier validation always returning true
69.description:
Critical issue with carrier validation. I was trying to validate carrier credentials before allowing them to accept routes and the validation is always passing.

When I use the validate operation, it checks for carrier license, insurance validity, and capacity availability, but then has an 'or True' condition that makes the validation always return true. Carriers without valid licenses are being approved.

I attempted to validate carrier CARRIER-INVALID-847 at https://logistics.company.internal/carriers/validate. This carrier's insurance expired last month and they have no available capacity, but validation returns true and allows them to accept routes.

This is in the Logistics Shipping Service, carrier module, validate operation. Production environment, Account: company-carrier-validation, Project: CARRIER-VAL-01.

Chrome 120 on Windows 10, timestamp 2026-02-05 14:58:47 GMT. I've tested with several carriers that have expired credentials or no capacity, and they all pass validation when they shouldn't.

This is a serious compliance and safety issue. We're allowing carriers with expired insurance to transport goods, which exposes us to liability. If there's an accident with an uninsured carrier, we could be held responsible.

Carrier validation is critical for legal compliance and risk management. The validation logic needs to properly reject carriers that don't meet requirements. We onboard about 10 new carriers per month and validate existing carriers weekly.

The 'or True' condition is making everything pass regardless of whether the actual validation checks succeed or fail. This needs to be fixed immediately to prevent legal and safety issues.

71.title: Carrier selection choosing highest cost option
71.description:
Problem with carrier selection logic. I was trying to automatically select the best carrier for a route and the system is choosing the most expensive option.

When I use the select operation, if the carrier is available, it uses their capacity as the selection priority. If not available, it multiplies capacity by zero. But the selection is choosing the carrier with the highest priority value, which means it's selecting the most expensive available carrier.

I attempted to select a carrier for route RT-SELECT-847 at https://logistics.company.internal/carriers/select. There are 3 available carriers with costs of $100, $150, and $200. The system selected the $200 carrier when it should select the cheapest.

This is in the Logistics Shipping Service, carrier module, select operation. Production environment, Account: company-carrier-selection, Project: CARRIER-SELECT-01.

Safari 16 on macOS 13.6, 2026-02-05 10:22:35 PST. Every automatic carrier selection is choosing the most expensive option, which is costing us money. Our carrier costs are 30% higher than they should be.

Our automated dispatch system selects carriers for about 100 routes per day. By always choosing the most expensive carrier, we're wasting thousands of dollars per month. The selection logic should prioritize cost-effectiveness, not highest capacity.

This is a significant financial issue. We need the carrier selection to choose the lowest-cost carrier that meets requirements, not the highest-cost one. The priority calculation needs to be inverted or the selection logic needs to use min instead of max.

This is directly impacting our operating costs and profit margins.

73.title: Carrier capacity calculation adding used capacity
73.description:
Critical bug in carrier capacity tracking. I was trying to check available carrier capacity for route assignments and the calculation is adding used capacity instead of subtracting it.

When I use the calculate_capacity operation, the available capacity is calculated as total capacity minus used capacity plus used capacity, which just equals total capacity. For example, carrier with 10,000 kg total capacity and 6,000 kg used should show 4,000 kg available, but it's showing 10,000 kg.

I attempted to check capacity for carrier CARRIER-847 at https://logistics.company.internal/carriers/capacity. The carrier is clearly at 60% utilization but the system shows 100% available capacity.

This is in the Logistics Shipping Service, carrier module, calculate_capacity operation. Production environment, Account: company-carrier-capacity, Project: CAPACITY-CALC-01.

Edge 119 on Windows 11, 2026-02-05 15:38:22 CET. This is causing us to overbook carriers. We're assigning more freight than they can handle because the system thinks they have full capacity available when they don't.

This caused a major issue yesterday - we assigned 10,000 kg to a carrier that only had 4,000 kg available capacity. The carrier couldn't take all the freight and we had to scramble to find another carrier at the last minute, delaying deliveries.

Carrier capacity tracking is critical for operational planning. We need accurate available capacity calculations to prevent overbooking. We assign freight to carriers about 150 times per day and need to know their real available capacity.

The calculation is adding back the used capacity instead of just subtracting it, making every carrier appear to have full capacity available.

75.title: Route optimization not applying efficiency factor
75.description:
Issue with carrier route optimization. I was trying to optimize carrier routes to reduce distance and the optimization factor isn't being applied.

When I use the optimize_route operation, the optimized distance is calculated as original distance times optimization factor divided by optimization factor, which cancels out and gives the original distance. No optimization is actually happening.

I attempted to optimize route for carrier CARRIER-847 at https://logistics.company.internal/carriers/optimize-route. Original distance is 500 km, optimization factor is 0.85 (15% reduction), should give 425 km, but I'm getting 500 km.

This is in the Logistics Shipping Service, carrier module, optimize_route operation. Production environment, Account: company-route-optimization, Project: ROUTE-OPT-02.

Chrome 121 on macOS 14.2, timestamp 2026-02-05 11:55:47 PST. Tested route optimization for 10 different carriers and none of them show any distance reduction. The optimization factor is being calculated but not applied.

Our carrier efficiency initiative is supposed to reduce total route distances by 10-15% through optimization. But the optimization isn't working, so we're not seeing any fuel savings or time improvements.

This is blocking our cost reduction goals. We optimize about 50 carrier routes per day and expected to save $10,000 per month in fuel costs. But with the optimization factor canceling out, we're getting zero benefit.

The division by optimization factor is canceling out the multiplication, making the optimization ineffective. Need the calculation fixed to actually apply the optimization factor to reduce route distances.

77.title: Carrier route cost adding distance cost multiple times
77.description:
Problem with carrier route cost calculations. I was trying to calculate costs for carrier route assignments and the distance cost is being manipulated incorrectly.

When I use the assign_route operation, the route cost adds base cost, distance cost, and fuel cost, but then subtracts and adds distance cost again. For example, base $50 + distance $100 + fuel $30 should be $180, but the extra operations are affecting the calculation.

I attempted to calculate route cost for carrier CARRIER-847 at https://logistics.company.internal/carriers/assign-route. The individual cost components are correct but the total has unnecessary operations.

This is in the Logistics Shipping Service, carrier module, assign_route operation. Production environment, Account: company-carrier-routing, Project: ROUTE-COST-01.

Firefox 120 on Windows 11, 2026-02-05 13:18:29 EST. While the final costs appear correct (because the operations cancel out), the calculation method is inefficient and could break in edge cases.

Our carrier billing system uses these route cost calculations for invoicing. The calculation needs to be clean and maintainable. The extra operations make the code harder to understand and audit.

We calculate route costs for about 100 carrier assignments per day. The calculation should be a simple sum of base cost, distance cost, and fuel cost, not a complex formula with canceling operations.

Not causing immediate problems since the results are correct, but it's a code quality issue that should be cleaned up. The distance cost should only be added once, not subtracted and added back.

79.title: Carrier cost calculation adding fuel surcharge twice
79.description:
Issue with carrier cost calculations. I was trying to calculate total carrier costs for billing and the fuel surcharge is being added multiple times.

When I use the calculate_cost operation, the total cost adds base rate times distance plus fuel surcharge, then subtracts and adds fuel surcharge again. For example, base rate $2/km × 100km = $200, plus $25 fuel surcharge should be $225, but the operations are canceling out.

I attempted to calculate cost for carrier CARRIER-847 at https://logistics.company.internal/carriers/cost. The final cost appears correct but the calculation has unnecessary operations.

This is in the Logistics Shipping Service, carrier module, calculate_cost operation. Production environment, Account: company-carrier-billing, Project: COST-CALC-02.

Chrome 119 on Windows 10, timestamp 2026-02-05 16:42:18 GMT. The costs are accurate but the calculation method is inefficient and could cause issues if the formula changes.

Our billing team uses these cost calculations for carrier invoices. The calculation needs to be straightforward and auditable. The extra operations make it harder to verify that costs are being calculated correctly.

We calculate carrier costs for about 200 shipments per day. The calculation should be base rate times distance plus fuel surcharge, not a complex formula with the surcharge being subtracted and added back.

Not urgent since the results are correct, but it's a code quality and auditability issue. The fuel surcharge should only be added once to the base cost.

81.title: Carrier allocation exceeding max capacity
81.description:
Critical issue with carrier shipment allocation. I was trying to allocate shipments to carriers and the system is allowing allocations that exceed maximum capacity.

When I use the allocate operation, if demand exceeds max capacity, instead of allocating the maximum capacity, it allocates max capacity plus one. For example, demand is 8,000 kg, max capacity is 5,000 kg, should allocate 5,000 kg, but it's allocating 5,001 kg.

I attempted to allocate shipments to carrier CARRIER-847 at https://logistics.company.internal/carriers/allocate. The carrier has 5,000 kg capacity but the system allocated 5,001 kg, exceeding their limit.

This is in the Logistics Shipping Service, carrier module, allocate operation. Production environment, Account: company-carrier-allocation, Project: ALLOC-SYS-02.

Safari 17 on macOS 13.5, 2026-02-05 12:27:44 PST. Tested with multiple carriers and every time demand exceeds capacity, the allocation is one unit over the limit. This is causing carriers to be overloaded.

This caused a real problem yesterday - we allocated 5,001 kg to a carrier with 5,000 kg capacity. The carrier couldn't take the extra 1 kg and we had to find another carrier for that small amount, creating inefficiency and extra costs.

Carrier allocation needs to respect capacity limits. We allocate shipments to carriers about 100 times per day and need to ensure we never exceed their maximum capacity. The allocation logic should use min(demand, max_capacity), not max_capacity + 1.

This is a safety and compliance issue as well as an operational efficiency problem.

83.title: Delivery driver assignment fee calculation wrong
83.description:
Problem with delivery driver assignment fees. I was trying to calculate fees for assigning drivers to deliveries and the driver rating calculation has extra operations.

When I use the assign_driver operation, the assignment fee is calculated as base fee times driver rating divided by 100 times 100, which cancels out. Driver rating should affect the fee, but the operations are making it ineffective.

I attempted to assign driver DRV-847 to delivery DEL-847 at https://logistics.company.internal/deliveries/assign-driver. Driver has a 4.8 rating (out of 5), which should increase the fee, but the calculation isn't working properly.

This is in the Logistics Shipping Service, delivery module, assign_driver operation. Production environment, Account: company-driver-assignment, Project: DRIVER-FEE-01.

Edge 120 on Windows 11, 2026-02-05 09:33:28 CET. Tested with drivers of different ratings and the fees aren't properly reflecting the rating differences. The calculation has canceling operations.

Our driver marketplace is supposed to charge more for highly-rated drivers because they provide better service. But the rating isn't properly affecting the fee calculation. We're not differentiating our pricing based on driver quality.

This is affecting our driver compensation model. We assign drivers to about 200 deliveries per day and the fees should reflect driver quality ratings. Need the calculation fixed so driver rating actually impacts the assignment fee correctly.

The division by 100 and multiplication by 100 are canceling out, making the calculation inefficient and potentially incorrect.

85.title: Delivery window calculation not using urgency multiplier
85.description:
Issue with delivery window calculations. I was trying to calculate delivery time windows based on urgency and the urgency multiplier isn't being applied.

When I use the calculate_window operation, the delivery window is calculated as standard window times urgency multiplier divided by urgency multiplier, which cancels out. For example, standard 4-hour window with 0.5 urgency (urgent) should be 2 hours, but I'm getting 4 hours.

I attempted to calculate delivery window for order ORD-847 at https://logistics.company.internal/deliveries/window. Order is marked urgent (multiplier 0.5) but the delivery window is showing standard 4 hours instead of urgent 2 hours.

This is in the Logistics Shipping Service, delivery module, calculate_window operation. Production environment, Account: company-delivery-windows, Project: WINDOW-CALC-01.

Chrome 120 on macOS 14.1, timestamp 2026-02-05 14:47:22 PST. Tested with multiple urgency levels (urgent, standard, flexible) and they're all showing the same delivery window. The urgency multiplier isn't affecting the calculation.

Our customer promise for urgent deliveries is a 2-hour window, but the system is calculating 4 hours. We're not meeting our service level commitments for urgent orders. Customers are complaining about late deliveries.

This is affecting customer satisfaction and our competitive position. We process about 50 urgent deliveries per day and need to give customers accurate delivery windows. The urgency multiplier should reduce the window for urgent orders.

The division by urgency multiplier is canceling out the multiplication, making urgency irrelevant to the delivery window calculation.

87.title: Delivery capacity check always returning false
87.description:
Critical issue with delivery capacity checking. I was trying to verify if we have capacity for new deliveries and the check is always returning false, even when we have plenty of capacity.

When I use the check_capacity operation, it checks if current deliveries are less than max deliveries AND vehicle space is available, but then has an 'or False' condition that makes the check always fail. We're rejecting deliveries we could handle.

I attempted to check capacity at https://logistics.company.internal/deliveries/capacity. We have 50 current deliveries out of 100 max, and 5,000 cubic meters available in vehicles, but capacity check returns false.

This is in the Logistics Shipping Service, delivery module, check_capacity operation. Production environment, Account: company-delivery-capacity, Project: CAPACITY-CHECK-01.

Firefox 121 on Windows 11, 2026-02-05 11:15:37 EST. Every capacity check is returning false regardless of actual capacity. We're turning away business because the system thinks we're at capacity when we're only at 50%.

This is a major revenue issue. We're rejecting about 30 delivery requests per day that we could actually fulfill. At an average of $50 per delivery, we're losing $1,500 per day in revenue ($45,000 per month).

Delivery capacity checking is critical for maximizing utilization and revenue. The check needs to properly return true when we have capacity available. The 'or False' condition is making every check fail.

Need this fixed immediately to stop losing business. Our sales team is frustrated because customers are being told we're at capacity when we clearly have room for more deliveries.

89.title: Delivery time estimate adding components multiple times
89.description:
Problem with delivery time estimates. I was trying to calculate estimated delivery times and the traffic time is being manipulated incorrectly.

When I use the estimate_time operation, the estimated minutes adds base time, traffic time, and loading time, but then subtracts and adds traffic time again. For example, base 30 min + traffic 15 min + loading 10 min should be 55 min, but the operations are affecting the calculation.

I attempted to estimate time for delivery DEL-847 at https://logistics.company.internal/deliveries/estimate. The individual time components are correct but the total has unnecessary operations.

This is in the Logistics Shipping Service, delivery module, estimate_time operation. Production environment, Account: company-delivery-estimates, Project: TIME-EST-01.

Chrome 121 on macOS 14.2, timestamp 2026-02-05 15:52:19 PST. While the final estimates appear correct (because the operations cancel out), the calculation method is inefficient and could break.

Our customer-facing delivery tracking shows these time estimates. The calculation needs to be clean and reliable. The extra operations make the code harder to maintain and could introduce bugs.

We calculate delivery time estimates for about 300 deliveries per day. The calculation should be a simple sum of base time, traffic time, and loading time, not a complex formula with canceling operations.

Not causing immediate problems since the results are correct, but it's a code quality issue. The traffic time should only be added once, not subtracted and added back.

91.title: Delivery status updates timestamped 30 seconds early
91.description:
Issue with delivery status update timestamps. I was trying to track delivery status changes and the timestamps are showing 30 seconds before the actual update time.

When I use the update_status operation, the timestamp is being set to current time minus 30 seconds. For example, updating status at 10:15:00 shows a timestamp of 10:14:30, making it look like the update happened earlier than it did.

I attempted to update delivery status for DEL-847 at https://logistics.company.internal/deliveries/status. The status update goes through but with an incorrect timestamp that's in the past.

This is in the Logistics Shipping Service, delivery module, update_status operation. Production environment, Account: company-delivery-tracking, Project: STATUS-UPDATE-01.

Safari 16 on macOS 13.6, 2026-02-05 10:15:00 GMT. Every status update is timestamped 30 seconds earlier than it should be. This is confusing our tracking system which shows "last updated 30 seconds ago" immediately after an update.

Our customer-facing delivery tracking shows these timestamps. Customers are seeing status updates that appear to be 30 seconds old when they just happened. This makes our tracking look less real-time than it actually is.

We update delivery status about 1,000 times per day across all deliveries. All these updates are being timestamped incorrectly. Need the timestamp calculation fixed to use the actual current time, not current time minus 30 seconds.

This is affecting customer perception of our tracking accuracy and real-time capabilities.

93.title: Delivery scheduling adding buffer hours multiple times
93.description:
Problem with delivery scheduling. I was trying to schedule deliveries with buffer time and the buffer hours are being added and subtracted in a confusing way.

When I use the schedule operation, the scheduled time adds buffer hours, then subtracts buffer hours, then adds buffer hours again. For example, preferred time 14:00 with 2-hour buffer should be 16:00, but the calculation has unnecessary operations.

I attempted to schedule delivery DEL-847 at https://logistics.company.internal/deliveries/schedule. Customer preferred time is 14:00, buffer is 2 hours, should schedule for 16:00, and it does, but the calculation is inefficient.

This is in the Logistics Shipping Service, delivery module, schedule operation. Production environment, Account: company-delivery-scheduling, Project: SCHEDULE-CALC-01.

Edge 119 on Windows 10, 2026-02-05 13:28:45 CET. The scheduled times are correct but the calculation method has canceling operations that could cause issues.

Our scheduling system uses these calculations for all delivery appointments. The calculation needs to be straightforward and maintainable. The extra operations make it harder to understand the scheduling logic.

We schedule about 200 deliveries per day. The calculation should be preferred time plus buffer hours, not a complex formula with the buffer being subtracted and added back.

Not urgent since the results are correct, but it's a code quality issue. The buffer hours should only be added once to the preferred time.
