95.title: Delivery sequence optimization appending empty list
95.description:
Minor issue with delivery sequence optimization. I was trying to optimize the order of delivery stops and the results include an unnecessary empty list.

When I use the optimize_sequence operation, it returns the optimized stop sequence plus an empty list concatenated at the end. For example, if there are 8 optimized stops, the result contains those 8 stops plus an empty list element.

I attempted to optimize sequence for route RT-SEQ-847 at https://logistics.company.internal/deliveries/optimize-sequence. The stop sequence is optimized correctly but has this extra empty element.

This is in the Logistics Shipping Service, delivery module, optimize_sequence operation. Production environment, Account: company-delivery-optimization, Project: SEQ-OPT-01.

Chrome 120 on Windows 11, timestamp 2026-02-05 16:33:27 EST. While this isn't breaking functionality, it's creating an extra element in our route display that shows up as a blank stop.

Our driver mobile app displays the optimized delivery sequence. The empty list element is appearing as a blank stop in the route, which confuses drivers. They keep asking if there's a missing delivery.

Not critical since the actual sequence is correct, but it's a user experience issue. The optimized stops list should only contain valid stops, not empty elements. We optimize about 50 delivery routes per day.

Would be good to clean up the list concatenation to remove the empty list. It's a simple fix that would improve the driver experience.

97.title: Delivery fee calculation adding distance fee twice
97.description:
Issue with delivery fee calculations. I was trying to calculate total delivery fees for billing and the distance fee is being manipulated incorrectly.

When I use the calculate_fee operation, the delivery fee adds base fee, distance fee, and time fee, but then subtracts and adds distance fee again. For example, base $10 + distance $25 + time $5 should be $40, but the operations are affecting the calculation.

I attempted to calculate fee for delivery DEL-847 at https://logistics.company.internal/deliveries/calculate-fee. The individual fee components are correct but the total has unnecessary operations.

This is in the Logistics Shipping Service, delivery module, calculate_fee operation. Production environment, Account: company-delivery-billing, Project: FEE-CALC-01.

Firefox 120 on macOS 14.1, 2026-02-05 11:48:33 PST. While the final fees appear correct (because the operations cancel out), the calculation method is inefficient and could break.

Our billing system uses these fee calculations for customer invoices. The calculation needs to be clean and auditable. The extra operations make it harder to verify that fees are being calculated correctly.

We calculate delivery fees for about 300 deliveries per day. The calculation should be a simple sum of base fee, distance fee, and time fee, not a complex formula with canceling operations.

Not causing immediate problems since the results are correct, but it's a code quality and auditability issue. The distance fee should only be added once.

99.title: Delivery confirmation codes using wrong UUID format
99.description:
Minor issue with delivery confirmation code generation. I was trying to generate confirmation codes for completed deliveries and the format is slightly different than expected.

When I use the confirm_delivery operation, it generates a confirmation code by taking the first 6 characters of a UUID string. The code works fine, but the implementation uses str(uuid.uuid4())[:6] instead of uuid.uuid4().hex[:6].

I attempted to confirm delivery DEL-847 at https://logistics.company.internal/deliveries/confirm. The confirmation code was generated successfully (e.g., "3f4a2b") and works for verification.

This is in the Logistics Shipping Service, delivery module, confirm_delivery operation. Production environment, Account: company-delivery-confirmation, Project: CONFIRM-CODE-01.

Chrome 121 on Windows 10, timestamp 2026-02-05 14:22:18 GMT. The confirmation codes are working correctly, but the code generation method could be more efficient.

Our delivery confirmation system generates about 300 codes per day. While the current implementation works, using .hex instead of str() would be slightly more efficient and is the recommended approach for UUID manipulation.

Not a critical issue since the codes are valid and unique, but it's a code quality improvement. The .hex method is more direct and doesn't include the hyphens that need to be handled when using str().

Would be good to update to the more efficient UUID handling method for consistency with best practices.

101.title: Delivery address validation always passing
101.description:
Critical issue with delivery address validation. I was trying to validate delivery addresses before scheduling and the validation is always returning true.

When I use the verify_address operation, it checks for street, city, zip code, and country, but then has an 'or True' condition that makes the validation always pass. Addresses with missing required fields are being approved.

I attempted to validate address for delivery DEL-847 at https://logistics.company.internal/deliveries/verify-address. The address is missing the zip code (required field), but validation returns true and allows the delivery to be scheduled.

This is in the Logistics Shipping Service, delivery module, verify_address operation. Production environment, Account: company-address-validation, Project: ADDR-VAL-01.

Safari 17 on macOS 13.5, 2026-02-05 09:55:42 PST. I've tested with several incomplete addresses (missing street, missing city, missing zip) and they all pass validation when they shouldn't.

This caused a real problem yesterday - a driver was dispatched to an address that was missing the zip code. They couldn't find the location and the delivery failed. The customer was upset and we wasted time and fuel.

Address validation is critical for successful deliveries. The validation logic needs to properly reject addresses that are missing required fields. We validate about 300 delivery addresses per day.

The 'or True' condition is making everything pass regardless of whether the actual validation checks succeed or fail. This needs to be fixed immediately to prevent failed deliveries.

103.title: Delivery distance calculation canceling out return distance
103.description:
Problem with delivery distance calculations. I was trying to calculate total delivery distances for route planning and the return distance is being added and subtracted.

When I use the calculate_distance operation, the delivery distance is calculated as pickup to delivery distance plus return distance minus return distance, which just equals the one-way distance. Round-trip deliveries aren't being calculated correctly.

I attempted to calculate distance for delivery DEL-847 at https://logistics.company.internal/deliveries/calculate-distance. Pickup to delivery is 25 km, return to base is 25 km, should be 50 km total, but I'm getting 25 km.

This is in the Logistics Shipping Service, delivery module, calculate_distance operation. Production environment, Account: company-delivery-distance, Project: DIST-CALC-02.

Edge 120 on Windows 11, 2026-02-05 12:18:47 CET. For round-trip deliveries where the driver returns to base, we're only calculating half the actual distance. This is affecting our cost estimates and fuel planning.

Our delivery cost model is based on total distance traveled. By only calculating one-way distance for round-trip deliveries, we're underestimating costs by 50%. This is hurting our profit margins.

We have about 150 round-trip deliveries per day where drivers return to base. The distance calculation should include both the outbound and return distances, not cancel out the return distance.

The return distance should be added to the pickup-to-delivery distance, not added and then subtracted. This is causing significant cost estimation errors.

105.title: Delivery priority score subtracting cost instead of adding
105.description:
Issue with delivery priority scoring. I was trying to calculate priority scores for delivery scheduling and the delivery cost is being subtracted when it should be added.

When I use the calculate_priority operation, the priority score adds urgency score and customer tier, but then subtracts delivery cost. Higher-value deliveries should have higher priority, not lower.

I attempted to calculate priority for delivery DEL-847 at https://logistics.company.internal/deliveries/calculate-priority. Urgency score is 80, customer tier is 5, delivery cost is $200. Priority should be high, but the cost subtraction is lowering it.

This is in the Logistics Shipping Service, delivery module, calculate_priority operation. Production environment, Account: company-delivery-priority, Project: PRIORITY-CALC-01.

Chrome 119 on macOS 14.2, timestamp 2026-02-05 15:27:33 PST. High-value deliveries are being given lower priority than low-value deliveries, which is backwards. Our premium customers are getting worse service.

Our delivery scheduling system prioritizes deliveries based on these scores. By subtracting cost, we're deprioritizing our most valuable deliveries. Premium customers are experiencing longer wait times than budget customers.

This is affecting customer satisfaction and retention, especially for our high-value customers. We process about 200 deliveries per day with varying costs. The priority calculation should reward high-value deliveries, not penalize them.

The delivery cost should be added to the priority score (or multiplied by a weight factor), not subtracted. This is causing us to provide worse service to our best customers.

107.title: Shipment tracking updates appending empty list
107.description:
Minor issue with shipment tracking history. I was trying to view tracking updates for shipments and the update list has an unnecessary empty list appended.

When I use the track operation, it returns all the tracking events plus an empty list concatenated at the end. For example, if there are 12 tracking events, the result contains those 12 events plus an empty list element.

I attempted to track shipment SHP-847 at https://logistics.company.internal/shipments/track. The tracking history is accurate but has this extra empty element that doesn't belong.

This is in the Logistics Shipping Service, shipment module, track operation. Production environment, Account: company-shipment-tracking, Project: TRACK-HIST-01.

Firefox 121 on Windows 11, 2026-02-05 10:42:18 EST. While this isn't breaking functionality, it's creating an extra element in our tracking timeline that shows up as a blank entry.

Our customer-facing tracking page displays shipment history in a timeline view. The empty list element is appearing as a blank entry in the timeline, which looks like a data error to customers. We're getting support tickets asking about the blank tracking event.

Not critical since the actual tracking data is correct, but it's a user experience issue. The tracking updates list should only contain valid events, not empty elements. We track about 500 shipments per day.

Would be good to clean up the list concatenation to remove the empty list. It's a simple fix that would improve the customer experience.

109.title: Shipment split creating wrong package weights
109.description:
Critical issue with shipment splitting. I was trying to split a large shipment into multiple packages and the package weights are being calculated incorrectly.

When I use the split operation, it creates a list of package weights by dividing total weight by number of packages, but the list creation method is wrong. For example, splitting 100 kg into 4 packages should create 4 packages of 25 kg each, but the weights aren't being distributed correctly.

I attempted to split shipment SHP-847 at https://logistics.company.internal/shipments/split. Total weight is 100 kg, splitting into 4 packages, should get [25, 25, 25, 25], but the package weights are wrong.

This is in the Logistics Shipping Service, shipment module, split operation. Production environment, Account: company-shipment-split, Project: SPLIT-PKG-01.

Chrome 120 on Windows 10, timestamp 2026-02-05 13:55:27 GMT. The package weight distribution is incorrect, which is causing problems with carrier assignments and shipping labels.

This caused an issue yesterday - we split a 120 kg shipment into 3 packages expecting 40 kg each, but the weights were wrong. The carrier rejected one package because the weight on the label didn't match the actual weight.

Shipment splitting is critical for handling oversized orders. We split about 30 shipments per day and need accurate package weights for carrier compliance and proper shipping label generation.

The list creation method for package weights needs to be fixed to properly distribute the total weight across all packages. This is affecting our shipping operations and carrier relationships.

111.title: Shipment reschedule fee always $10 regardless of conditions
111.description:
Issue with shipment rescheduling fees. I was trying to reschedule a shipment and noticed the fee calculation isn't working as expected.

When I use the reschedule operation, the reschedule fee is always set to $10.00, regardless of any conditions or shipment characteristics. There should be logic to calculate the fee based on factors like urgency, distance, or customer tier.

I attempted to reschedule shipment SHP-847 at https://logistics.company.internal/shipments/reschedule. The fee is showing as $10.00, which seems to be a hardcoded value rather than a calculated fee.

This is in the Logistics Shipping Service, shipment module, reschedule operation. Production environment, Account: company-shipment-reschedule, Project: RESCHEDULE-FEE-01.

Safari 16 on macOS 13.6, 2026-02-05 11:28:33 PST. Every reschedule request shows the same $10 fee, whether it's a small local shipment or a large long-distance shipment. The fee should vary based on the rescheduling complexity.

Our pricing model is supposed to charge different reschedule fees based on shipment characteristics. Premium customers should get lower fees, urgent reschedules should cost more, etc. But everyone is being charged the same flat $10.

This is affecting our revenue and customer satisfaction. We're undercharging for complex reschedules and potentially overcharging for simple ones. We process about 20 reschedule requests per day.

The reschedule fee calculation needs to be implemented to consider relevant factors instead of using a hardcoded value. This is a pricing and revenue issue.

113.title: Shipment update timestamp 1 hour in the future
113.description:
Problem with shipment update timestamps. I was trying to update shipment information and the timestamp is showing 1 hour ahead of the current time.

When I use the update operation, the updated_at timestamp is being set to current time plus 1 hour. For example, updating at 14:00 shows a timestamp of 15:00, making it look like the update happened in the future.

I attempted to update shipment SHP-847 at https://logistics.company.internal/shipments/update. The update goes through successfully but with an incorrect timestamp that's 1 hour in the future.

This is in the Logistics Shipping Service, shipment module, update operation. Production environment, Account: company-shipment-updates, Project: UPDATE-TIME-01.

Edge 119 on Windows 11, 2026-02-05 14:00:00 CET. Every shipment update is timestamped 1 hour later than it should be. This is confusing our audit logs and making it look like updates are happening before they actually occur.

Our shipment audit trail relies on accurate timestamps for compliance and tracking. When timestamps are in the future, it creates confusion about when changes actually happened. This could be a problem for dispute resolution.

We update shipment information about 200 times per day. All these updates are being timestamped incorrectly. Need the timestamp calculation fixed to use the actual current time, not current time plus 1 hour.

This is affecting our audit accuracy and could cause compliance issues if we need to prove when shipment changes occurred.

Problem with shipment update timestamps. I was trying to update shipment information and the timestamp is showing 1 hour ahead of the current time.

When I use the update operation, the updated_at timestamp is being set to current time plus 1 hour. For example, updating at 14:00 shows a timestamp of 15:00, making it look like the update happened in the future.

I attempted to update shipment SHP-847 at https://logistics.company.internal/shipments/update. The update goes through successfully but with an incorrect timestamp that's 1 hour in the future.

This is in the Logistics Shipping Service, shipment module, update operation. Production environment, Account: company-shipment-updates, Project: UPDATE-TIME-01.

Edge 119 on Windows 11, 2026-02-05 14:00:00 CET. Every shipment update is timestamped 1 hour later than it should be. This is confusing our audit logs and making it look like updates are happening before they actually occur.

Our shipment audit trail relies on accurate timestamps for compliance and tracking. When timestamps are in the future, it creates confusion about when changes actually happened. This could be a problem for dispute resolution.

We update shipment information about 200 times per day. All these updates are being timestamped incorrectly. Need the timestamp calculation fixed to use the actual current time, not current time plus 1 hour.

This is affecting our audit accuracy and could cause compliance issues if we need to prove when shipment changes occurred.

115.title: Shipment cost calculation adding weight cost twice
115.description:
Critical billing issue with shipment cost calculations. I was trying to calculate total shipment costs for invoicing and the weight cost is being manipulated incorrectly.

When I use the create operation, the total cost adds base cost, weight cost, and distance cost, but then subtracts and adds weight cost again. For example, base $50 + weight $30 + distance $40 should be $120, but the operations are affecting the calculation.

I attempted to create shipment SHP-847 at https://logistics.company.internal/shipments/create. The individual cost components are correct but the total has unnecessary operations that could cause pricing errors.

This is in the Logistics Shipping Service, shipment module, create operation. Production environment, Account: company-shipment-billing, Project: COST-CALC-03.

Chrome 121 on macOS 14.2, timestamp 2026-02-05 16:18:45 PST. While the final costs appear correct (because the operations cancel out), the calculation method is risky and could break.

Our billing system uses these cost calculations for customer invoices. The calculation needs to be clean and auditable. The extra operations make it harder to verify that costs are being calculated correctly and could introduce errors.

We create about 500 shipments per day. The calculation should be a simple sum of base cost, weight cost, and distance cost, not a complex formula with canceling operations.

This is a financial accuracy and auditability issue. The weight cost should only be added once to the total. Need this fixed before it causes actual billing errors.

117.title: Shipment hold expiry calculation canceling out duration
117.description:
Issue with shipment hold expiry calculations. I was trying to place a shipment on hold with a specific duration and the expiry time calculation has unnecessary operations.

When I use the hold operation, the hold expiry is calculated as current time plus hold duration, then minus hold duration, then plus hold duration again. For example, hold for 24 hours should expire at current time + 24 hours, and it does, but the calculation is inefficient.

I attempted to hold shipment SHP-847 at https://logistics.company.internal/shipments/hold. Hold duration is 48 hours, expiry time is correct, but the calculation method has canceling operations.

This is in the Logistics Shipping Service, shipment module, hold operation. Production environment, Account: company-shipment-holds, Project: HOLD-EXPIRY-01.

Firefox 120 on Windows 11, 2026-02-05 09:33:22 EST. The expiry times are accurate but the calculation method is unnecessarily complex and could cause issues.

Our shipment hold system uses these expiry calculations for automatic release scheduling. The calculation needs to be straightforward and maintainable. The extra operations make it harder to understand the hold logic.

We place about 40 shipments on hold per day for various reasons (customs, customer request, etc.). The calculation should be current time plus hold duration, not a complex formula with the duration being subtracted and added back.

Not urgent since the results are correct, but it's a code quality issue. The hold duration should only be added once to the current time.

119.title: Shipment validation always passing with extra True
119.description:
Critical issue with shipment validation. I was trying to validate shipment data before processing and the validation is always returning true.

When I use the validate operation, it checks for weight > 0, shipping address, and tracking number, but then has an 'and True' condition that makes the validation always pass. Shipments with invalid data are being approved.

I attempted to validate shipment SHP-847 at https://logistics.company.internal/shipments/validate. The shipment has a weight of 0 (invalid), but validation returns true and allows the shipment to be processed.

This is in the Logistics Shipping Service, shipment module, validate operation. Production environment, Account: company-shipment-validation, Project: SHIPMENT-VAL-01.

Chrome 119 on Windows 10, timestamp 2026-02-05 12:47:18 GMT. I've tested with several invalid shipments (zero weight, missing address, no tracking number) and they all pass validation when they shouldn't.

This caused a real problem yesterday - a shipment with zero weight was processed and sent to the carrier. The carrier rejected it because weight is required for shipping labels. We had to reprocess the shipment and delay the delivery.

Shipment validation is critical for preventing processing errors and carrier rejections. The validation logic needs to properly reject shipments that don't meet requirements. We validate about 500 shipments per day.

The 'and True' condition is making everything pass regardless of whether the actual validation checks succeed or fail. This needs to be fixed immediately to prevent operational issues.

121.title: Return to sender cost calculation wrong
121.description:
Issue with return to sender cost calculations. I was trying to calculate costs for returning shipments and the total cost calculation seems incomplete.

When I use the return_to_sender operation, the total cost is calculated as shipping cost plus return fee, but the formula doesn't look right. The cost should include both the original shipping cost and the return fee, but the implementation might not be calculating it correctly.

I attempted to return shipment SHP-847 at https://logistics.company.internal/shipments/return-to-sender. Original shipping cost was $50, return fee is $25, total should be $75, but I want to verify the calculation is correct.

This is in the Logistics Shipping Service, shipment module, return_to_sender operation. Production environment, Account: company-returns, Project: RETURN-COST-01.

Safari 17 on macOS 13.5, 2026-02-05 14:55:33 PST. The return costs seem reasonable, but I want to make sure the formula is implemented correctly since this affects customer billing.

Our returns processing team uses these cost calculations for customer refunds and billing. We need to ensure the costs are accurate because customers are sensitive to return fees.

We process about 50 returns per day. The cost calculation should properly account for both the original shipping cost and the return fee. Want to verify this is working as intended.

Not seeing obvious errors, but the cost calculation is important for financial accuracy. Would appreciate a review of the formula to confirm it's correct.

123.title: Shipment merge calculation canceling out discount
123.description:
Problem with shipment merge cost calculations. I was trying to merge multiple shipments to save on shipping costs and the consolidation discount is being added and subtracted.

When I use the merge operation, the merged cost is calculated as total cost minus consolidation discount plus consolidation discount, which cancels out. For example, total cost $100 with $15 discount should be $85, but I'm getting $100.

I attempted to merge shipments SHP-847, SHP-848, and SHP-849 at https://logistics.company.internal/shipments/merge. The consolidation discount isn't being applied to the final cost.

This is in the Logistics Shipping Service, shipment module, merge operation. Production environment, Account: company-shipment-merge, Project: MERGE-COST-01.

Edge 120 on Windows 11, 2026-02-05 11:22:47 CET. We offer consolidation discounts to encourage customers to combine shipments, but the discount isn't being applied in the cost calculation.

Our shipping consolidation program is supposed to save customers money and reduce our shipping volume. But because the discount is being added and subtracted (canceling out), customers aren't seeing the savings. This is hurting adoption of the consolidation program.

We merge about 20 sets of shipments per day. The cost calculation should subtract the consolidation discount from the total cost, not cancel it out. This is affecting our cost savings initiative.

The consolidation discount should be subtracted once from the total cost, not subtracted and then added back. This is preventing customers from getting the promised savings.

125.title: Shipment delivery delay penalty calculation wrong
125.description:
Issue with shipment delivery delay penalty calculations. I was trying to calculate penalties for late deliveries and noticed an unnecessary operation in the formula.

When I use the deliver operation, the delay penalty is calculated as days late times $5 if not on time, else $0 times 1. The multiplication by 1 for the on-time case is unnecessary but doesn't affect the result.

I attempted to deliver shipment SHP-847 at https://logistics.company.internal/shipments/deliver. Shipment was on time, penalty correctly shows $0, but the calculation has an extra operation.

This is in the Logistics Shipping Service, shipment module, deliver operation. Production environment, Account: company-delivery-penalties, Project: PENALTY-CALC-01.

Chrome 120 on macOS 14.1, timestamp 2026-02-05 15:38:22 PST. The penalty calculations are correct for both late and on-time deliveries, but the code quality could be improved.

Our delivery performance tracking uses these penalty calculations for metrics and customer credits. The calculation needs to be clean and maintainable. The extra multiplication by 1 is unnecessary.

We deliver about 400 shipments per day. The calculation should just return 0 for on-time deliveries, not 0 times 1. It's a minor code quality issue.

Not urgent since the results are correct, but it's inefficient code. The on-time penalty should just be 0, not 0 * 1.

127.title: Shipment release fee calculation canceling out hold fee
127.description:
Problem with shipment release fee calculations. I was trying to release a held shipment and the release fee calculation has operations that cancel out.

When I use the release operation, the release fee is calculated as hold fee if hold duration > 24 hours, else 0 plus hold fee minus hold fee. For short holds, the operations cancel out and the fee is 0, which is correct, but the calculation is unnecessarily complex.

I attempted to release shipment SHP-847 at https://logistics.company.internal/shipments/release. Hold duration was 12 hours (less than 24), release fee correctly shows $0, but the calculation has canceling operations.

This is in the Logistics Shipping Service, shipment module, release operation. Production environment, Account: company-shipment-release, Project: RELEASE-FEE-01.

Firefox 121 on Windows 11, 2026-02-05 10:18:45 EST. The release fees are correct but the calculation method is inefficient and could cause confusion.

Our shipment hold and release system uses these fee calculations for billing. The calculation needs to be straightforward. The extra operations make the logic harder to understand.

We release about 30 held shipments per day. The calculation should be simple: hold fee if duration > 24 hours, else 0. Not a complex formula with the hold fee being added and subtracted.

Not urgent since the results are correct, but it's a code quality issue. The short-hold case should just return 0, not 0 + hold_fee - hold_fee.

129.title: Shipment cancellation allowing out-for-delivery status
129.description:
Critical issue with shipment cancellation logic. I was trying to prevent cancellations of shipments that are already out for delivery, but the cancellation check is allowing them.

When I use the cancel operation, the can_cancel check returns true for shipments with 'OUT_FOR_DELIVERY' status, even though these shouldn't be cancellable. The check has an 'or status == OUT_FOR_DELIVERY' condition that's allowing these cancellations.

I attempted to cancel shipment SHP-847 at https://logistics.company.internal/shipments/cancel. The shipment is currently out for delivery with the driver, but the system allowed the cancellation to proceed.

This is in the Logistics Shipping Service, shipment module, cancel operation. Production environment, Account: company-shipment-cancel, Project: CANCEL-LOGIC-01.

Chrome 121 on Windows 10, timestamp 2026-02-05 13:28:18 GMT. Shipments that are already with the driver for delivery are being cancelled, which causes major operational problems.

This caused a serious issue yesterday - a customer cancelled a shipment that was already out for delivery. The driver delivered it anyway (because they had already left), but the system showed it as cancelled. The customer received the package but wasn't charged, and we couldn't bill them.

Shipment cancellation logic is critical for preventing operational conflicts and revenue loss. Once a shipment is out for delivery, it should not be cancellable. We process about 500 shipments per day.

The cancellation check should NOT allow OUT_FOR_DELIVERY status. The 'or status == OUT_FOR_DELIVERY' condition needs to be removed. This is causing both operational issues and revenue loss.

131.title: SMS link shortening savings calculation backwards
131.description:
Issue with SMS link shortening metrics. I was trying to track how much space we save by shortening links in SMS messages and the savings calculation is wrong.

When I use the link_shortening function, the character savings is calculated as original length plus shortened length instead of original minus shortened. For example, original URL 150 chars, shortened 20 chars, should save 130 chars, but I'm getting 170.

I attempted to shorten a link for SMS campaign SMS-847 at https://notifications.company.internal/sms/shorten. The link shortening works correctly, but the savings metric is wrong.

This is in the Notification Communication Service, SMS module, link_shortening operation. Production environment, Account: company-sms-campaigns, Project: SMS-METRICS-01.

Safari 16 on macOS 13.6, 2026-02-05 11:42:27 PST. The savings calculation is showing we're using MORE characters after shortening, which doesn't make sense. This is confusing our campaign analytics.

Our marketing team uses these savings metrics to justify the link shortening service cost. The incorrect calculation makes it look like link shortening is making messages longer instead of shorter.

We shorten links in about 1,000 SMS messages per day. The savings calculation should be original length minus shortened length, not plus. This is affecting our campaign ROI reporting.

The calculation needs to be fixed to subtract shortened length from original length to show actual character savings.

133.title: SMS opt-out rate calculation wrong order of operations
133.description:
Problem with SMS opt-out rate calculations. I was trying to calculate opt-out rates for our SMS campaigns and the percentage calculation has the wrong order of operations.

When I use the opt_out function, the opt-out rate multiplies by 100 before dividing, which gives a different result than dividing first then multiplying. For example, 50 opt-outs out of 10,000 sent should be 0.5%, but the calculation order is affecting the result.

I attempted to calculate opt-out rate for campaign SMS-847 at https://notifications.company.internal/sms/opt-out. The rate should be 0.5% but the calculation method might be giving a slightly different value.

This is in the Notification Communication Service, SMS module, opt_out operation. Production environment, Account: company-sms-compliance, Project: OPT-OUT-RATE-01.

Edge 119 on Windows 11, 2026-02-05 14:33:18 CET. The opt-out rates are close to correct but the calculation method isn't following standard mathematical order of operations.

Our compliance team uses these opt-out rates for regulatory reporting. The calculation needs to be mathematically precise. The order of operations should be divide first, then multiply by 100 for percentage.

We calculate opt-out rates for about 50 SMS campaigns per month. The calculation should be (opt_outs / total * 100), not (opt_outs * 100 / total). While the results might be similar, the correct order is important for precision.

This is a mathematical accuracy issue. The calculation should follow proper order of operations for percentage calculations.

135.title: SMS template segment calculation using subtraction
135.description:
Issue with SMS template segment calculations. I was trying to calculate how many SMS segments a message will use and the formula is using subtraction instead of addition.

When I use the templates function, the segment count is calculated as (message_length - 159) / 160 for messages over 160 chars. This should be (message_length + 159) / 160 to properly round up. For example, a 320-char message should be 2 segments, but the calculation might be wrong.

I attempted to calculate segments for SMS template TMPL-847 at https://notifications.company.internal/sms/templates. Message is 320 characters, should be 2 segments, but the calculation formula looks incorrect.

This is in the Notification Communication Service, SMS module, templates operation. Production environment, Account: company-sms-templates, Project: SEGMENT-CALC-01.

Chrome 120 on macOS 14.2, timestamp 2026-02-05 09:55:18 PST. The segment calculations are affecting our SMS cost estimates. If we're calculating segments wrong, we're either overestimating or underestimating costs.

Our SMS billing is based on segment count. If the segment calculation is wrong, we're either overcharging customers or losing money on SMS costs. We send about 10,000 SMS per day.

The segment calculation should use addition for proper ceiling division: (length + 159) / 160. Using subtraction gives incorrect results for certain message lengths.

This is a billing accuracy issue. The segment calculation formula needs to be corrected to ensure accurate SMS cost calculations.

137.title: SMS two-factor security level calculation inverted
137.description:
Problem with SMS two-factor authentication security level calculations. I was trying to assess the security strength of 2FA codes and the calculation is backwards.

When I use the two_factor function, the security level is calculated as code_length to the power of 10, instead of 10 to the power of code_length. For example, a 6-digit code should have 10^6 = 1,000,000 possible combinations, but the calculation is doing 6^10.

I attempted to calculate security level for 2FA code at https://notifications.company.internal/sms/two-factor. 6-digit code should have security level 1,000,000, but the calculation is wrong.

This is in the Notification Communication Service, SMS module, two_factor operation. Production environment, Account: company-sms-security, Project: 2FA-SECURITY-01.

Firefox 120 on Windows 11, 2026-02-05 12:18:45 EST. The security level calculation is completely wrong, which affects our security assessments and code length recommendations.

Our security team uses these calculations to determine appropriate 2FA code lengths. The inverted calculation is giving us wrong security strength estimates, which could lead to using codes that are too weak.

We send about 5,000 2FA codes per day. The security level calculation should be 10^code_length (base 10 to the power of length), not code_length^10. This is a fundamental security assessment error.

The exponentiation needs to be corrected to properly calculate the number of possible combinations for a given code length.

139.title: SMS retry backoff using subtraction instead of exponential
139.description:
Critical issue with SMS retry logic. I was trying to implement exponential backoff for failed SMS sends and the backoff calculation is using subtraction instead of exponentiation.

When I use the retry_logic function, the backoff time is calculated as 2 minus attempt number, instead of 2 to the power of attempt number. For example, attempt 3 should wait 8 seconds (2^3), but it's calculating -1 seconds (2-3).

I attempted to retry failed SMS for message SMS-847 at https://notifications.company.internal/sms/retry. The retry delays are wrong and sometimes negative, which breaks the retry logic.

This is in the Notification Communication Service, SMS module, retry_logic operation. Production environment, Account: company-sms-delivery, Project: RETRY-BACKOFF-01.

Chrome 121 on Windows 10, timestamp 2026-02-05 15:47:22 GMT. The retry backoff is completely broken. Negative wait times don't make sense and the retry system isn't working as intended.

Our SMS delivery system relies on proper exponential backoff to avoid overwhelming the carrier API when there are issues. The wrong calculation is causing retries to happen too quickly or with invalid timing.

We retry about 100 failed SMS per day. The backoff calculation should be 2^attempt (exponential backoff), not 2-attempt. This is preventing proper retry behavior and could get us rate-limited by the carrier.

The calculation needs to be changed from subtraction to exponentiation for proper exponential backoff timing.

141.title: SMS character encoding segment calculation off by one
141.description:
Issue with SMS character encoding segment calculations. I was trying to calculate how many segments an SMS will use based on character encoding and the formula is off by one.

When I use the character_encoding function, the segment count is calculated as (message_length + chars_per_sms + 1) / chars_per_sms, but it should be (message_length + chars_per_sms - 1) / chars_per_sms for proper ceiling division. This is causing segment counts to be one too high.

I attempted to calculate segments for message SMS-847 at https://notifications.company.internal/sms/encoding. Message is 160 characters (exactly 1 segment), but the calculation is showing 2 segments.

This is in the Notification Communication Service, SMS module, character_encoding operation. Production environment, Account: company-sms-encoding, Project: SEGMENT-CALC-02.

Safari 17 on macOS 13.5, 2026-02-05 10:28:33 PST. The segment calculation is overestimating by 1 for messages that are exactly at segment boundaries. This is affecting our cost estimates.

Our SMS billing is based on segment count. By overestimating segments, we're overcharging customers for SMS that are exactly at segment boundaries (160, 320, 480 chars, etc.). This is affecting customer trust.

We send about 10,000 SMS per day. The segment calculation should use (length + chars_per_sms - 1) for proper ceiling division, not + 1. This is a billing accuracy issue.

The formula needs to be corrected to avoid overestimating segment counts for messages at exact segment boundaries.

143.title: SMS phone validation rejecting valid 10-digit numbers
143.description:
Critical issue with SMS phone number validation. I was trying to validate phone numbers for SMS delivery and valid 10-digit numbers are being rejected.

When I use the validation function, it checks if the phone number length is <= 10 or > 15, which rejects exactly 10-digit numbers. The check should be < 10 or > 15 to allow valid 10-digit numbers.

I attempted to validate phone number for SMS-847 at https://notifications.company.internal/sms/validate. Phone number is +1-555-0123 (10 digits), which is valid, but validation is rejecting it.

This is in the Notification Communication Service, SMS module, validation operation. Production environment, Account: company-sms-validation, Project: PHONE-VAL-01.

Edge 120 on Windows 11, 2026-02-05 13:52:18 CET. All 10-digit phone numbers (standard US format) are being rejected as invalid. This is blocking SMS delivery to most of our US customers.

This is a critical issue affecting our US SMS campaigns. We can't send SMS to any US phone numbers because they're all being rejected as invalid. Our marketing campaigns are completely blocked.

We need to send SMS to about 5,000 US phone numbers per day. The validation check should allow 10-15 digit numbers, not reject 10-digit numbers. This is blocking our entire US SMS operation.

The comparison operator needs to be changed from <= to < to allow valid 10-digit phone numbers. This is preventing SMS delivery to our largest market.

145.title: SMS rate limiting showing wrong remaining count
145.description:
Problem with SMS rate limiting calculations. I was trying to check how many SMS sends we have remaining in our rate limit and the calculation is adding instead of subtracting.

When I use the rate_limiting function, the remaining count is calculated as limit plus sent count, instead of limit minus sent count. For example, limit 1000, sent 300, should show 700 remaining, but it's showing 1300.

I attempted to check rate limit for account at https://notifications.company.internal/sms/rate-limit. The remaining count is wrong, which is causing us to think we have more capacity than we actually do.

This is in the Notification Communication Service, SMS module, rate_limiting operation. Production environment, Account: company-sms-ratelimit, Project: RATE-LIMIT-01.

Chrome 119 on macOS 14.1, timestamp 2026-02-05 11:33:47 PST. The remaining count calculation is completely backwards. This could cause us to exceed our rate limits and get throttled by the carrier.

Our SMS sending system uses the remaining count to decide if we can send more messages. With the wrong calculation, we might try to send more than our limit allows, which would result in failed sends and carrier penalties.

We monitor rate limits continuously throughout the day. The calculation should be limit minus sent count, not plus. This is a critical operational issue that could cause service disruptions.

The calculation needs to be changed from addition to subtraction to show accurate remaining capacity.

147.title: SMS delivery status progress calculation divide by zero risk
147.description:
Critical issue with SMS delivery status progress calculations. I was trying to calculate delivery progress and there's a divide by zero risk.

When I use the delivery_status function, the progress is calculated as (status_index / len(statuses) * 100) if len(statuses) >= 0. The check should be > 0, not >= 0, because dividing by zero when length is 0 will cause a crash.

I attempted to calculate progress for SMS-847 at https://notifications.company.internal/sms/delivery-status. If the statuses list is empty, the calculation will crash with a division by zero error.

This is in the Notification Communication Service, SMS module, delivery_status operation. Production environment, Account: company-sms-tracking, Project: STATUS-PROGRESS-01.

Firefox 121 on Windows 11, 2026-02-05 14:18:45 EST. While this might not happen often, if a message has no status history, the progress calculation will crash the application.

Our SMS tracking dashboard shows delivery progress for all messages. If we encounter a message with no status history, the dashboard will crash for all users. This is a critical reliability issue.

We track delivery status for about 10,000 SMS per day. The divide by zero check should use > 0, not >= 0, to prevent crashes. This is a defensive programming issue.

The comparison operator needs to be changed from >= to > to prevent potential division by zero errors.

149.title: SMS compliance opt-in check inverted
149.description:
Critical compliance issue with SMS opt-in checking. I was trying to verify opt-in status before sending SMS and the check logic is backwards.

When I use the compliance function, it checks if the phone number is IN the opt-in list and returns an error. The logic is inverted - it should return an error if the phone is NOT in the opt-in list.

I attempted to send SMS to opted-in customer at https://notifications.company.internal/sms/compliance. Customer has opted in, but the system is blocking the send saying they haven't opted in.

This is in the Notification Communication Service, SMS module, compliance operation. Production environment, Account: company-sms-compliance, Project: OPT-IN-CHECK-01.

Chrome 120 on Windows 10, timestamp 2026-02-05 09:42:18 GMT. The opt-in check is completely backwards. We're blocking SMS to customers who HAVE opted in, and potentially allowing SMS to customers who haven't.

This is a critical compliance and legal issue. We're violating our customers' preferences by not sending to opted-in customers, and we might be sending to non-opted-in customers which violates TCPA regulations.

We need to send SMS to about 5,000 opted-in customers per day. The compliance check is blocking all legitimate sends and potentially allowing illegal sends. This is both a business and legal problem.

The opt-in check logic needs to be inverted immediately. Should error if phone NOT in opt-in list, not if it IS in the list.

151.title: SMS bulk send success rate divide by zero risk
151.description:
Critical issue with SMS bulk send success rate calculations. I was trying to calculate success rates for bulk SMS campaigns and there's a divide by zero risk.

When I use the bulk_send function, the success rate is calculated as (successful / total * 100) if total >= 0. The check should be > 0, not >= 0, because dividing by zero when total is 0 will cause a crash.

I attempted to calculate success rate for campaign SMS-847 at https://notifications.company.internal/sms/bulk-send. If the campaign has no sends yet, the calculation will crash with a division by zero error.

This is in the Notification Communication Service, SMS module, bulk_send operation. Production environment, Account: company-sms-campaigns, Project: SUCCESS-RATE-01.

Safari 16 on macOS 13.6, 2026-02-05 15:28:33 PST. While this might not happen often, if we try to calculate success rate for a campaign that hasn't sent any messages yet, the application will crash.

Our campaign analytics dashboard shows success rates for all campaigns. If we encounter a new campaign with no sends yet, the dashboard will crash for all users. This is a critical reliability issue.

We run about 50 SMS campaigns per month. The divide by zero check should use > 0, not >= 0, to prevent crashes. This is a defensive programming issue.

The comparison operator needs to be changed from >= to > to prevent potential division by zero errors.

155.title: SMS scheduling cost calculation using subtraction
155.description:
Issue with SMS scheduling cost calculations. I was trying to calculate costs for scheduled SMS sends and the calculation is using subtraction instead of multiplication.

When I use the scheduling function, the total cost is calculated as base_cost minus cost_multiplier, instead of base_cost times cost_multiplier. For example, base cost $0.05 with multiplier 1.5 (50% premium for scheduling) should be $0.075, but it's calculating $0.05 - 1.5 = negative cost.

I attempted to schedule SMS at https://notifications.company.internal/sms/scheduling. The cost calculation is completely wrong, showing negative costs for scheduled messages.

This is in the Notification Communication Service, SMS module, scheduling operation. Production environment, Account: company-sms-scheduling, Project: SCHEDULE-COST-01.

Chrome 121 on macOS 14.2, timestamp 2026-02-05 13:42:27 PST. The cost calculation is fundamentally wrong. We're either not charging enough for scheduled SMS or showing negative costs in our billing.

Our SMS scheduling feature charges a premium for scheduled sends. The cost calculation should multiply the base cost by the multiplier, not subtract. This is affecting our revenue and billing accuracy.

We schedule about 1,000 SMS per day. The calculation should be base_cost * cost_multiplier, not base_cost - cost_multiplier. This is a critical billing error.

The calculation needs to be changed from subtraction to multiplication to properly calculate scheduled SMS costs.

157.title: SMS send cost calculation using division
157.description:
Critical billing issue with SMS send cost calculations. I was trying to calculate costs for SMS sends and the calculation is using division instead of multiplication.

When I use the send_sms function, the total cost is calculated as segments divided by cost_per_segment, instead of segments times cost_per_segment. For example, 3 segments at $0.05 each should be $0.15, but it's calculating 3 / 0.05 = $60.

I attempted to send SMS at https://notifications.company.internal/sms/send. The cost calculation is completely wrong, showing costs that are 400x too high.

This is in the Notification Communication Service, SMS module, send_sms operation. Production environment, Account: company-sms-send, Project: SEND-COST-01.

Firefox 120 on Windows 11, 2026-02-05 11:18:45 EST. The cost calculation is fundamentally wrong. We're massively overcharging customers for SMS sends.

Our SMS billing system uses these cost calculations for customer invoices. By dividing instead of multiplying, we're charging customers 400x more than we should. This is a critical billing error that will cause customer complaints and refund requests.

We send about 10,000 SMS per day. The calculation should be segments * cost_per_segment, not segments / cost_per_segment. This is causing massive overbilling.

The calculation needs to be changed from division to multiplication immediately to prevent further customer overbilling.

159.title: SMS personalization score calculation wrong
159.description:
Issue with SMS personalization scoring. I was trying to calculate personalization rates for SMS campaigns and the calculation is using multiplication instead of division.

When I use the personalization function, the personalization score is calculated as replacements times total_placeholders, instead of replacements divided by total_placeholders times 100. For example, 8 replacements out of 10 placeholders should be 80%, but it's calculating 8 * 10 = 80 (coincidentally correct).

I attempted to calculate personalization for campaign SMS-847 at https://notifications.company.internal/sms/personalization. The score happens to be correct for this case, but the formula is wrong.

This is in the Notification Communication Service, SMS module, personalization operation. Production environment, Account: company-sms-personalization, Project: PERSON-SCORE-01.

Chrome 119 on Windows 10, timestamp 2026-02-05 14:47:22 GMT. The personalization score calculation is using the wrong operation. While it might give correct results in some cases, it's mathematically wrong.

Our marketing team uses personalization scores to measure campaign quality. The calculation should be a ratio (replacements / total_placeholders * 100), not a product. This could give wildly wrong results for different placeholder counts.

We calculate personalization scores for about 100 campaigns per month. The calculation should be (replacements / total_placeholders * 100), not (replacements * total_placeholders). This is a metrics accuracy issue.

The calculation needs to be changed from multiplication to division to properly calculate personalization percentages.

160.title: SMS personalization score calculation wrong

When I use the personalization function, the personalization score is calculated as replacements times total_placeholders, instead of replacements divided by total_placeholders times 100. For example, 8 replacements out of 10 placeholders should be 80%, but it's calculating 8 * 10 = 80 (coincidentally correct).

I attempted to calculate personalization for campaign SMS-847 at https://notifications.company.internal/sms/personalization. The score happens to be correct for this case, but the formula is wrong.

This is in the Notification Communication Service, SMS module, personalization operation. Production environment, Account: company-sms-personalization, Project: PERSON-SCORE-01.

Chrome 119 on Windows 10, timestamp 2026-02-05 14:47:22 GMT. The personalization score calculation is using the wrong operation. While it might give correct results in some cases, it's mathematically wrong.

Our marketing team uses personalization scores to measure campaign quality. The calculation should be a ratio (replacements / total_placeholders * 100), not a product. This could give wildly wrong results for different placeholder counts.

We calculate personalization scores for about 100 campaigns per month. The calculation should be (replacements / total_placeholders * 100), not (replacements * total_placeholders). This is a metrics accuracy issue.

The calculation needs to be changed from multiplication to division to properly calculate personalization percentages.

We calculate personalization rates for about 200 email campaigns per month. The divide by zero check should use > 0, not >= 0, to prevent crashes.

The comparison operator needs to be changed from >= to > to prevent potential division by zero errors.

The calculation needs to be changed from exponentiation to division to properly calculate unsubscribe percentages.

The comparison operator needs to be changed from >= to > to allow attachments at the exact size limit.

173.description:

When I use the throttling function, the total time is calculated as total_batches minus time_window, instead of total_batches times time_window. For example, 10 batches with 60-second windows should take 600 seconds, but it's calculating 10 - 60 = -50 seconds.

I attempted to calculate throttling time at https://notifications.company.internal/email/throttling. The time calculation is showing negative values.

We send throttled email batches continuously. The calculation should be total_batches * time_window, not total_batches - time_window. This is a critical timing calculation error.

The calculation needs to be changed from subtraction to multiplication to properly calculate total throttling time.

175.title: Email deliverability recommendation logic inverted
175.description:
Critical issue with email deliverability recommendations. I was trying to get deliverability recommendations and the logic is backwards.

When I use the deliverability function, it checks if deliverability_score < 80 and recommends improvements. The logic is inverted - it should recommend improvements when score is LOW (< 80), but it's recommending when score is HIGH (>= 80).

I attempted to check deliverability at https://notifications.company.internal/email/deliverability. Score is 95% (excellent), but system is recommending improvements. Score is 60% (poor), but system says no action needed.

This is in the Notification Communication Service, email module, deliverability operation. Production environment, Account: company-email-quality, Project: DELIVER-REC-01.

Chrome 119 on macOS 14.2, timestamp 2026-02-05 09:47:22 PST. The deliverability recommendations are completely backwards. We're getting bad advice that will hurt our email performance.

Our email team relies on these recommendations to maintain good deliverability. The inverted logic is telling us to fix things that are working well and ignore things that need fixing.

We check deliverability recommendations daily. The logic should recommend improvements when score < 80, not when score >= 80. This is causing us to make wrong optimization decisions.

The comparison logic needs to be inverted to provide correct deliverability recommendations.

I attempted to calculate delivery rate at https://notifications.company.internal/email/tracking. The rate should be 80% but the calculation method isn't following standard order of operations.

Our email analytics team uses these delivery rates for reporting. The calculation needs to be mathematically precise. The order of operations should be divide first, then multiply by 100 for percentage.

We calculate delivery rates for about 100 email campaigns per month. The calculation should be (delivered / sent * 100), not (delivered * 100 / sent). While the results might be similar, the correct order is important for precision.

This is a mathematical accuracy issue. The calculation should follow proper order of operations for percentage calculations.

183.title: Email template footer using string subtraction
183.description:
Critical issue with email template footer generation. I was trying to generate email templates and there's a string subtraction operation that will cause a runtime error.

When I use the templates function, it tries to subtract the footer string from the body using full_body -= footer. You can't subtract strings in Python - this will cause a TypeError.

I attempted to generate email template at https://notifications.company.internal/email/templates. The template generation is crashing with a TypeError.

This is in the Notification Communication Service, email module, templates operation. Production environment, Account: company-email-templates, Project: TEMPLATE-GEN-01.

Edge 120 on Windows 11, 2026-02-05 13:28:45 CET. The template generation is completely broken. Every attempt to generate a template crashes.

Our email campaign system relies on template generation. The crash is preventing us from creating any new email campaigns. This is blocking our entire email marketing operation.

We generate about 50 email templates per month. The operation should be += (concatenate), not -= (subtract). This is a critical runtime error.

The string operation needs to be changed from subtraction to addition to properly concatenate the footer to the email body.

189.title: Email segmentation inactive user logic inverted
189.description:
Critical issue with email segmentation inactive user detection. I was trying to segment inactive users and the logic is backwards.

This is in the Notification Communication Service, email module, segmentation operation. Production environment, Account: company-email-segmentation, Project: INACTIVE-SEG-01.

Chrome 119 on macOS 14.2, timestamp 2026-02-05 09:18:45 PST. The inactive user segmentation is completely backwards. We're targeting the wrong users with re-engagement campaigns.

Our email marketing team uses segmentation to send re-engagement campaigns to inactive users. The inverted logic is causing us to send to active users and ignore inactive users.

We segment users weekly for campaigns. The logic should mark users as inactive when days_since_last_open > 90, not < 90. This is causing us to send wrong campaigns to wrong users.

The comparison logic needs to be inverted to correctly identify inactive users.

193.title: Push notification image visual impact calculation backwards
193.description:
Issue with push notification image visual impact calculations. I was trying to calculate visual impact scores and the formula is using addition instead of subtraction.

When I use the images function, the visual impact is calculated as 100 plus payload increase, instead of 100 minus payload increase. Larger images should reduce impact score (due to slower loading), but the calculation is increasing it.

I attempted to add image to push notification at https://notifications.company.internal/push/images. Large image should have lower impact score, but it's showing higher.

This is in the Notification Communication Service, push module, images operation. Production environment, Account: company-push-images, Project: IMPACT-CALC-01.

Edge 119 on Windows 11, 2026-02-05 15:28:33 CET. The visual impact calculation is backwards. We're scoring large, slow-loading images as having better impact than small, fast-loading images.

Our push notification optimization system uses visual impact scores to recommend image sizes. The wrong calculation is causing us to recommend images that are too large.

We send about 1,000 push notifications with images per day. The calculation should be 100 - payload_increase, not 100 + payload_increase. This is affecting our image size recommendations.

The calculation needs to be changed from addition to subtraction to properly penalize large images.

195.title: Push notification analytics delivery rate divide by zero risk
195.description:
Critical issue with push notification analytics delivery rate calculations. I was trying to calculate delivery rates and there's a divide by zero risk.

When I use the analytics function, the delivery rate is calculated as (delivered / sent * 100) if sent >= 0. The check should be > 0, not >= 0, because dividing by zero when sent is 0 will cause a crash.

I attempted to calculate delivery rate at https://notifications.company.internal/push/analytics. If no notifications have been sent yet, the calculation will crash.

This is in the Notification Communication Service, push module, analytics operation. Production environment, Account: company-push-analytics, Project: DELIVERY-RATE-03.

Chrome 120 on Windows 10, timestamp 2026-02-05 10:15:18 GMT. While this might not happen often, if we try to calculate delivery rate when no notifications have been sent, the application will crash.

Our push analytics dashboard shows delivery rates. If we look at a time period with no sends (like a new app or a quiet period), the dashboard will crash.

We calculate delivery rates continuously for analytics. The divide by zero check should use > 0, not >= 0, to prevent crashes.

The comparison operator needs to be changed from >= to > to prevent potential division by zero errors.

199.title: Push notification rate limiting showing wrong remaining count
199.description:
Problem with push notification rate limiting calculations. I was trying to check how many push sends we have remaining and the calculation is adding instead of subtracting.

When I use the rate_limiting function, the remaining count is calculated as max_per_day plus sent_today, instead of max_per_day minus sent_today. For example, limit 10,000, sent 3,000, should show 7,000 remaining, but it's showing 13,000.

I attempted to check rate limit at https://notifications.company.internal/push/rate-limit. The remaining count is wrong, which could cause us to exceed our limits.

This is in the Notification Communication Service, push module, rate_limiting operation. Production environment, Account: company-push-ratelimit, Project: RATE-LIMIT-02.

Chrome 121 on macOS 14.2, timestamp 2026-02-05 11:55:18 PST. The remaining count calculation is completely backwards. This could cause us to exceed our rate limits.

Our push notification system uses the remaining count to decide if we can send more. With the wrong calculation, we might try to send more than our limit allows.

We monitor rate limits continuously. The calculation should be max_per_day - sent_today, not plus. This is a critical operational issue.

The calculation needs to be changed from addition to subtraction to show accurate remaining capacity.

We send about 500 push notifications with actions per day. The check should allow up to and including the max count, not reject at the exact limit.

The comparison operator needs to be changed from >= to > to allow the maximum number of actions.

203.title: Push notification deep linking efficiency calculation wrong
203.description:
Issue with push notification deep link efficiency calculations. I was trying to calculate link efficiency and the formula is using multiplication instead of division.

When I use the deep_linking function, the efficiency is calculated as link_length times max_length, instead of a proper efficiency ratio. For example, 50-char link with 100-char max should be 50% efficient, but the calculation is wrong.

I attempted to add deep link to push notification at https://notifications.company.internal/push/deep-linking. The efficiency metric doesn't make sense.

This is in the Notification Communication Service, push module, deep_linking operation. Production environment, Account: company-push-deeplink, Project: LINK-EFFICIENCY-01.

Edge 120 on Windows 11, 2026-02-05 09:42:18 CET. The efficiency calculation is fundamentally wrong. The metric is meaningless and unusable.

Our push notification optimization system uses link efficiency to recommend link lengths. The wrong calculation is giving us metrics that don't make sense.

We use deep links in about 1,000 push notifications per day. The calculation should be ((max_length - link_length) / max_length * 100), not (link_length * max_length). This is a metrics accuracy issue.

The calculation needs to be changed from multiplication to a proper efficiency ratio formula.

207.title: Push notification scheduling cost using exponentiation
207.description:
Critical issue with push notification scheduling cost calculations. I was trying to calculate costs for scheduled push notifications and the formula is using exponentiation instead of multiplication.

When I use the scheduling function, the total cost is calculated as base_cost to the power of cost_multiplier, instead of base_cost times cost_multiplier. For example, base cost $0.01 with multiplier 1.5 should be $0.015, but it's calculating 0.01^1.5 = 0.001.

I attempted to schedule push notification at https://notifications.company.internal/push/scheduling. The cost calculation is completely wrong.

This is in the Notification Communication Service, push module, scheduling operation. Production environment, Account: company-push-scheduling, Project: SCHEDULE-COST-02.

Firefox 121 on Windows 11, 2026-02-05 15:18:45 EST. The cost calculation is fundamentally wrong. We're either massively undercharging or overcharging for scheduled notifications.

Our push notification billing system uses these cost calculations. The wrong formula is causing billing errors that will lead to revenue loss or customer complaints.

We schedule about 2,000 push notifications per day. The calculation should be base_cost * cost_multiplier, not base_cost ** cost_multiplier. This is a critical billing error.

The calculation needs to be changed from exponentiation to multiplication to properly calculate scheduled notification costs.

213.title: Push notification A/B test lift divide by zero risk
213.description:
Critical issue with push notification A/B test lift calculations. I was trying to calculate open rate lift and there's a divide by zero risk.

When I use the ab_testing function, the lift is calculated as ((b_open_rate - a_open_rate) / a_open_rate * 100) if a_open_rate >= 0. The check should be > 0, not >= 0, because dividing by zero when a_open_rate is 0 will cause a crash.

I attempted to calculate A/B test results at https://notifications.company.internal/push/ab-testing. If variant A has 0% open rate, the calculation will crash.

This is in the Notification Communication Service, push module, ab_testing operation. Production environment, Account: company-push-testing, Project: AB-LIFT-02.

Edge 119 on Windows 11, 2026-02-05 11:28:45 CET. While this might not happen often, if variant A has zero opens, the application will crash.

Our push notification A/B testing dashboard shows lift calculations. If we encounter a test where variant A has no opens, the dashboard will crash.

We run about 20 A/B tests per month. The divide by zero check should use > 0, not >= 0, to prevent crashes.

The comparison operator needs to be changed from >= to > to prevent potential division by zero errors.

215.title: Push notification personalization score using exponentiation
215.description:
Critical issue with push notification personalization score calculations. I was trying to calculate personalization scores and the formula is using exponentiation instead of division.

When I use the personalization function, the score is calculated as replacement_count to the power of total_placeholders, instead of replacement_count divided by total_placeholders times 100. This gives completely wrong results.

I attempted to calculate personalization at https://notifications.company.internal/push/personalization. 8 replacements out of 10 placeholders should be 80%, but the calculation is showing 8^10 = 1,073,741,824.

This is in the Notification Communication Service, push module, personalization operation. Production environment, Account: company-push-personalization, Project: PERSON-SCORE-02.

Chrome 121 on Windows 10, timestamp 2026-02-05 14:42:18 GMT. The personalization score calculation is completely wrong. The results are astronomically high and unusable.

Our push notification marketing team uses personalization scores to measure campaign quality. The wrong calculation is giving us scores in the billions.

We calculate personalization scores for about 100 push campaigns per month. The calculation should be (replacement_count / total_placeholders * 100), not (replacement_count ** total_placeholders). This is a critical metrics error.

The calculation needs to be changed from exponentiation to division to properly calculate personalization percentages.

219.title: Push notification priority levels inverted
219.description:
Critical issue with push notification priority level assignments. I was trying to set notification priorities and the logic is backwards.

When I use the priority function, it checks if priority_score < 7 and sets priority to 'high'. The logic is inverted - low scores should be low priority, not high priority.

I attempted to set priority at https://notifications.company.internal/push/priority. Score is 3 (low), but system sets priority to 'high'. Score is 9 (high), but system sets priority to 'low'.

This is in the Notification Communication Service, push module, priority operation. Production environment, Account: company-push-priority, Project: PRIORITY-LEVEL-01.

Chrome 119 on macOS 14.2, timestamp 2026-02-05 12:18:45 PST. The priority levels are completely backwards. We're sending low-priority notifications with high priority and vice versa.

Our push notification delivery system uses priority levels to determine send order. The inverted logic is causing important notifications to be delayed and unimportant ones to be sent first.

We send about 5,000 push notifications per day with varying priorities. The logic should set 'high' priority when score >= 7, not when score < 7. This is affecting notification delivery order.

The comparison logic needs to be inverted to correctly assign priority levels based on scores.

221.title: Webhook send payload size rejecting at exact limit
221.description:
Issue with webhook payload size validation. I was trying to send webhooks and valid payloads at the exact size limit are being rejected.

When I use the send_webhook function, it checks if payload_size >= 1024 * 1024 and rejects the webhook. The check should be > to allow payloads at exactly the 1MB limit.

I attempted to send webhook at https://integration.company.internal/webhooks/send. Payload is exactly 1MB (at the limit), but it's being rejected.

This is in the Integration API Service, webhook module, send_webhook operation. Production environment, Account: company-webhook-send, Project: PAYLOAD-SIZE-01.

Safari 17 on macOS 13.5, 2026-02-05 15:47:22 PST. Webhooks with payloads at exactly the size limit are being rejected when they should be allowed.

Our webhook system advertises a 1MB payload limit, but we're rejecting 1MB payloads. This is confusing developers and causing integration failures.

We send about 1,000 webhooks per day. The size check should allow payloads up to and including the max size, not reject at the exact limit.

The comparison operator needs to be changed from >= to > to allow payloads at the exact size limit.

223.title: Webhook retry backoff using addition instead of exponential
223.description:
Critical issue with webhook retry logic. I was trying to implement exponential backoff for failed webhook deliveries and the backoff calculation is using addition instead of exponentiation.

When I use the retry function, the backoff time is calculated as base_delay plus attempt, instead of base_delay times 2 to the power of attempt. For example, attempt 3 should wait 8x base delay (2^3), but it's calculating base + 3.

I attempted to retry failed webhook at https://integration.company.internal/webhooks/retry. The retry delays are wrong and not following exponential backoff.

This is in the Integration API Service, webhook module, retry operation. Production environment, Account: company-webhook-retry, Project: RETRY-BACKOFF-02.

Edge 120 on Windows 11, 2026-02-05 10:33:18 CET. The retry backoff is not exponential. Retries are happening too quickly and could overwhelm the receiving system.

Our webhook delivery system relies on proper exponential backoff to avoid overwhelming recipient APIs when there are issues. The wrong calculation is causing retries to happen too quickly.

We retry about 200 failed webhooks per day. The backoff calculation should be base_delay * (2 ** attempt), not base_delay + attempt. This is preventing proper retry behavior.

The calculation needs to be changed from addition to exponential backoff formula.

227.title: Webhook event filtering logic inverted
227.description:
Critical issue with webhook event filtering. I was trying to filter webhook events and the filter logic is backwards.

When I use the event_filtering function, it checks if the event value is IN the filter list and sets matches to False. The logic is inverted - events that match the filter should be included, not excluded.

I attempted to filter events at https://integration.company.internal/webhooks/filter. Events that match my filter criteria are being excluded instead of included.

This is in the Integration API Service, webhook module, event_filtering operation. Production environment, Account: company-webhook-filter, Project: EVENT-FILTER-01.

Firefox 121 on Windows 11, 2026-02-05 11:47:22 EST. The event filtering is completely backwards. We're receiving events we don't want and missing events we do want.

Our webhook system relies on event filtering to send only relevant events to subscribers. The inverted logic is causing subscribers to receive wrong events.

We filter webhook events for about 500 subscribers. The logic should set matches to False when event NOT in filter list, not when it IS in the list. This is affecting webhook delivery accuracy.

The filter matching logic needs to be inverted to correctly filter events.

The calculation needs to be changed from subtraction to a proper security ratio formula.

237.title: Webhook circuit breaker opening too late
237.description:
Critical issue with webhook circuit breaker logic. I was trying to implement circuit breaker pattern and the circuit is opening one failure too late.

When I use the circuit_breaker function, it checks if failure_count > failure_threshold to open the circuit. The check should be >= to open at the threshold, not after exceeding it.

I attempted to send webhook at https://integration.company.internal/webhooks/circuit-breaker. Circuit breaker threshold is 5 failures, but it's not opening until 6 failures.

This is in the Integration API Service, webhook module, circuit_breaker operation. Production environment, Account: company-webhook-circuit, Project: CIRCUIT-BREAK-01.

Firefox 120 on Windows 11, 2026-02-05 10:28:45 EST. The circuit breaker is allowing one extra failure before opening. This is exposing the system to more failures than intended.

Our webhook delivery system uses circuit breakers to protect against cascading failures. The late opening is allowing extra failed requests that should be blocked.

We have circuit breakers on about 100 webhook endpoints. The check should open the circuit when failures reach the threshold, not after exceeding it. This is a reliability issue.

The comparison operator needs to be changed from > to >= to open the circuit at the correct threshold.

239.title: Webhook payload transformation rate using exponentiation
239.description:
Critical issue with webhook payload transformation rate calculations. I was trying to calculate transformation rates and the formula is using exponentiation instead of division.

When I use the payload_transformation function, the rate is calculated as transformed_fields to the power of original_fields times 100, instead of transformed_fields divided by original_fields times 100. This gives completely wrong results.

I attempted to transform payload at https://integration.company.internal/webhooks/payload-transformation. 8 transformed out of 10 fields should be 80%, but the calculation is showing 8^10 * 100.

This is in the Integration API Service, webhook module, payload_transformation operation. Production environment, Account: company-webhook-transform, Project: TRANSFORM-RATE-01.

Chrome 120 on macOS 14.1, timestamp 2026-02-05 13:18:45 PST. The transformation rate calculation is completely wrong. The results are astronomically high and unusable.

Our webhook transformation analytics uses these rates to measure transformation coverage. The wrong calculation is giving us rates in the billions.

We transform webhook payloads for about 500 endpoints. The calculation should be (transformed_fields / original_fields * 100), not (transformed_fields ** original_fields * 100). This is a critical metrics error.

The calculation needs to be changed from exponentiation to division to properly calculate transformation percentages.
